Weights

* All figures are rough estimates for planning purposes only — use SimBrief for more accurate final calculations. Adj load figures are weight-corrected at sea level; per-airport density-altitude correction is applied in the matrix tooltips and dimming, not here.

Range scaling model

Adj range = base × ln(TOW / ZFW) ÷ ln(MTOW / ZFWpub)

ZFW = OEW + payload; ZFWpub = MTOW − max fuel. Breguet range equation, per-airframe OEW & fuel data.

Performance suppressed when payload + fuel exceeds MTOW. TO & LDG use weight² for all types.

SimFly Active Player-Owned Airports
✈  New Pilot Codes

?  How to Use This Tool
✈
Point-to-Point Distance
Type any two ICAO codes into the Departure and Arrival fields to instantly calculate the great-circle distance in nautical miles. The result appears to the right of the inputs. Fields turn red if an ICAO isn't found in the database. Press Enter or Tab to move between fields; Escape clears both.
🗺️
File on SimBrief
Once both departure and arrival ICAOs are valid, the File on SimBrief → button lights up. Click it to open SimBrief's dispatch page pre-filled with your route — ready to generate a full flight plan in one click.

Hover the button to preview exactly what will be sent: origin, destination, aircraft type (if one is selected), and flight number. The flight number is synthesized from the 3-letter owner codes of the departure and arrival airport owners.
✈️
Aircraft Panel
Click the Aircraft header to expand the panel. Use the category tabs to filter by aircraft type, then click any chip to select an aircraft. The spec strip shows takeoff distance, landing distance, and range — both at full MTOW and adjusted for your current fuel and payload settings.

The Fuel slider sets fuel load as a percentage of max capacity. The Payload slider sets passenger/cargo load as a percentage of max payload. Both sliders feed into the adjusted performance column and affect the suitability check. The stacked Weights bar shows OEW, payload, and fuel as proportions of MTOW.

When an aircraft is selected, the Filter airports unsuitable for aircraft button appears below the matrix. Click it to dim any airport the selected aircraft cannot use — based on runway length, surface type (paved vs. unpaved), SimFly category limits, and whether the destination is within range from the current departure. The filter re-evaluates automatically whenever you change the departure airport or adjust the fuel or payload sliders.

Your selection is remembered between sessions — the aircraft, fuel%, and payload% are saved to localStorage the moment you change them, so the app opens with your last setup intact. Launching from the Recip Tracker (Plan Flight or Send N to AA) still overrides the saved state for that session.

Density-altitude correction — required runway distance is automatically scaled for density altitude using a per-category factor (light pistons feel DA the most, jets the least). When you've fetched weather, the airport's METAR temperature and altimeter setting drive the calculation; otherwise the app falls back to ISA conditions from the field elevation alone. The suitability tooltip shows the DA used and its source — e.g. DA 11,400 ft (METAR, 14:30Z) or DA 9,228 ft (ISA — fetch weather for accuracy). Cold or low-pressure airports never reduce required distance below book numbers.
ℹ️
Airport Info & External Links
Hover over any ICAO label — in the matrix, or in the airport name lines below the inputs — to see a popup with the airport's full name, IATA code, city, country, elevation, and runway details (lengths, surfaces, and lighting).

The airport name lines are also clickable links to the SimFly airport details page. Small icon buttons beside each name open Flightsim.to and FS Addon Compare search results for that ICAO in a new tab. Tip — hold Shift to open these in the background.
⛅
Weather & Flight Conditions
The departure and arrival airports auto-fetch a METAR the moment you set or change either ICAO, and again whenever you return to the tab if their cached data is older than 30 minutes — so the route endpoints are always current without you doing anything. Click the ⛅ button in the top-left corner of the distance matrix to fetch live METAR data for every other airport currently in view in one batch. Each ICAO label gets a colored dot showing its flight category: VFR (green), MVFR (blue), IFR (red), or LIFR (magenta). Hover any dot to see the full decoded report — conditions, wind, visibility, ceiling, observation time, and raw METAR text.

If an airport has no reporting station of its own, the nearest one within 100 nm is used as a fallback, shown with a dashed ring around the dot. Weather data is cached for 30 minutes; switching owner dropdowns clears the cache and prompts a fresh fetch. If the Distance Map panel is open when you fetch, map marker rings update to show flight categories too. Distance Map info cards below the map also include a Weather section with the same decoded report whenever METAR data is cached for the highlighted route's airports.
📋
Owner Distance Matrix
Select a Departure Airport Owner and an Arrival Airport Owner from the dropdowns to build a full distance matrix — every departure airport on the rows, every arrival airport on the columns. The brightest yellow cell marks the longest route currently visible within the active distance filter range.

Click any distance cell to highlight its row and column and populate the Departure and Arrival fields above with those ICAOs.

The ⇄ button between the owner dropdowns swaps the Departure and Arrival selections instantly.
↕️
Row & Column Sorting
Every row and column header in the matrix has a small ⊙ pivot dot on its right side. Click the dot to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport — clicking ⊙ on a departure row sorts the arrival columns by distance from that dep airport; clicking ⊙ on an arrival column sorts the departure rows by distance to that arr airport.

Each click on the same pivot dot advances the mode: ⊙ (alphabetical) → ▲ Nearest first → ▼ Farthest first → ⊙ (cleared). The active pivot dot is highlighted so you can always see which airport the sort is anchored to. Clicking the dot on a different airport switches the pivot to that one.

Row and column highlighting is independent of sorting. Click a row label or column header to highlight it and populate the Departure / Arrival ICAO fields above — this does not change the sort. To restore alphabetical order, click the active pivot dot until it cycles back to ⊙.

Tip — hovering a pivot dot shows a tooltip describing what the next click will do (e.g. "Sort dep airports by distance to KSEA").
🎚️
Distance Filter
The dual-handle slider below the matrix filters routes by distance. Drag the left handle to set a minimum and the right handle to set a maximum. Routes outside the range are dimmed; routes within range remain fully visible. The longest route within the active range is highlighted in yellow. You can also drag the filled bar itself to pan the range window without changing its width.
🏷️
Category Filter
The numbered circle buttons (1–7) below the distance slider filter the matrix by SimFly airport category. They form two independent groups — Dep filters departure airports (matrix rows) and Arr filters arrival airports (matrix columns). All categories are shown by default. Click a circle to toggle that category off or on for its side — airports in hidden categories are dimmed in both their ICAO labels and their distance cells. All categories can be toggled off simultaneously.

The Inv button at the end of each group inverts that group's selection — active categories become inactive and vice versa, including toggling all off when all are on.

Enable Hide filtered airports (below the circles) to remove airports from the matrix entirely rather than dimming them. This applies to airports in hidden categories, and also to any row or column where every route falls outside the active distance filter range. Row and column highlights on hidden airports are cleared automatically.
🌍
Route Map
Click the ROUTE MAP header below the matrix to expand the interactive map panel. Every departure and arrival airport appears as a pin — departure pins use a dark fill, arrival pins use a lighter fill. Clicking any pin zooms and pans the map to show the full set of visible airports at once.

When a distance cell is active (row and column highlighted), an airport info strip appears below the map with the full details for both airports. If weather has been fetched, each pin's ring shows its flight category color — a legend in the map corner explains the scheme.

Use the 🛰 Satellite button in the top-right corner of the map to toggle between satellite imagery and street map view.
⊙
Map
Click ⊙ Map in the panel header to open the interactive map. All SimFly player-owned airports are shown as color-coded dots (matching each airport’s SimFly category color). Hover any dot to see a tooltip with the ICAO, category, airport name, owner, and longest runway details after a brief delay. Once the map is open, click ⊙ Select Regions in its toolbar to start the cross-region distance matrix workflow (everything below describes that workflow).

Step 1 — Departure region: click and hold anywhere on the map to place the departure center, then drag outward to draw a circle. The radius line and live nm distance guide your selection. Release to lock the region. If an aircraft is selected in the main UI, a dashed gold ring shows its effective range from that center.

Step 2 — Arrival region: after releasing, the cursor switches to ruler mode — moving the mouse shows the distance from the departure center. Click and drag again to draw the arrival region circle the same way.

Cancelling mid-draw: right-click anywhere on the map during the region-drawing sequence (before or after the departure is locked, while drawing the arrival circle) to instantly cancel the operation and return to the explore state. Equivalent to clicking the Undo / Cancel button without having to move the cursor off the map.

Once both regions are set, a results bar appears with controls arranged around the centered route count. On the left: Sort order, a Dep Owners ▾ dropdown, and the Dep category filter group. On the right: the Arr category filter group, an Arr Owners ▾ dropdown, and the Max per region cap. The two owner menus filter independently — use Dep Owners to restrict departure rows and Arr Owners to restrict arrival columns. Each menu has a search box at the top: type any letters to instantly narrow the list to matching owners; press Enter to clear the search and return to the full list (selections are preserved). While search text is active, All and None apply only to the visible rows — hidden rows keep their current state. Use All or None with no search text to select or clear the complete list, or toggle individual owners by clicking their row. The matrix and map dots update in real time as you filter. If at least one owner is hidden, moving or resizing a region preserves that selection — hidden owners stay hidden, and any new owners entering the region appear visible by default. If all owners are visible, the list auto-refreshes when the region changes.

Map dot selection — click any airport dot on the map to build a route. Behavior depends on whether the matrix is visible: before both regions are drawn (or for clicks outside both region circles), it follows a fixed-departure anchor model — the first click sets the departure, the next click sets the arrival, subsequent clicks replace the arrival, and re-clicking the current departure clears the entire route. Once both regions are drawn and the matrix is visible, clicking inside the dep region always sets / changes the departure, and clicking inside the arr region always sets / changes the arrival — order doesn’t matter, so you can adjust either endpoint by clicking another in-region airport at any time. Use the ✕ Clear route button to reset. Overlap airports (inside both circles) toggle to whichever slot they aren’t already in. Selecting a departure/arrival pair this way immediately updates the main screen departure and arrival fields and triggers owner auto-selection — no separate Send to Flight Planner click needed for that. Double-click any dot to zoom the map to that airport instead; double-clicking does not affect the route selection.

Unique Arrivals mode — the small Venn-icon button in the results bar (next to Max per region) is a three-state cycle: Off → ↗ Far → ↘ Near → Off. When active, the matrix keeps every departure row but hides any arrival column whose airport also appears in the departure region, so the grid shows only routes that land at airports unique to the arr region. Map dots are not affected — every airport in either region stays visible, so you can still route via dot clicks. While the mode is active, Max per region applies asymmetrically: the dep cap always keeps the N airports closest to the dep center, but the arr cap follows whichever direction is selected — ↗ Far (default on activation) keeps the N airports farthest from the arr center (favours the edge of the arr region where overlap with dep is least likely, so more truly-unique arrivals survive the column filter); ↘ Near keeps the N airports closest to the arr center instead. The current direction shows directly on the button as a coloured badge, and the tooltip explains what the next click will do. The icon shows an asymmetric Venn: dep circle outlined on the left, arr-minus-dep crescent filled in the arrival orange on the right when active. Below ~1500 px the “Unique Arrivals” label is hidden and only the Venn icon + direction badge show; the tooltip still explains the effect.

Crosses antimeridian — when a route, measurement, or dep→arr region distance crosses ±180° longitude (e.g. New Zealand to Hawaii), Leaflet draws the visual line the long way across the map even though the displayed distance is the short-way figure. In that case the on-map distance label shows a second row reading Crosses antimeridian, and the line itself switches to a dashed style so the apparent mismatch is explained at a glance.

Map category filter — the Map filter buttons at the top of the region screen also control the main screen departure and arrival category filters: toggling a category on the map instantly applies the same selection to both the Dep and Arr filter rows on the main screen.

Airport owner filter — the Airport Owners ▾ dropdown in the header bar filters which owners’ airports appear as dots on the map. It operates independently of the Dep Owners and Arr Owners controls (which affect only the matrix rows and columns) — use Airport Owners to declutter the map to just the pilots you care about while leaving the matrix unaffected. The panel behaves identically to the dep/arr owner menus: type to search, press Enter to clear the search, use All / None to bulk-select, arrow keys to navigate, and Space to toggle a highlighted row. Hovering the dropdown shows a color legend listing every owner currently in the filter with their assigned dot color.

Owner-colored map dots — the 🎨 button immediately to the right of the Airport Owners dropdown switches the map dots from category / payout colors to a unique per-owner color. The button is greyed out until at least one owner is selected; once enabled, click it to engage or disengage owner coloring. The mode chip in the filter strip (Category Colors (n) / Payout Colors (n)) extends to a three-way cycle when owner filtering is active: category → payout → owner → category. Up to 16 selected owners each get a maximally-distinct curated color; above 16 owners the assignment falls back to the hash-based palette used by the region-matrix owner stripes.

Sticky headers — departure row labels stay pinned to the left edge and arrival column headers stay pinned to the top as you scroll a large matrix, keeping airport identifiers visible at all times.

Owner stripe — each row and column header shows a thin colored ribbon for the airport owner. Click the ribbon to instantly filter the matrix to that owner only; a second click restores the full list.

Drag the thin handle between the map and the results bar to resize the map vs. matrix area. Click any matrix cell to select a route; the Send to Flight Planner → button closes the modal and populates the main fields.

Airport search — a search box floats in the top-left corner of the map. Type an ICAO code, airport name, city, country name, region / state / province name (California, Bavaria, Ontario, …), or continent name to find airports. Owner rows (⊙), continent rows (🌍), country rows (🌍), and region rows (📍) show Filter map → and filter the map to matching SimFly-owned airports. Selecting an individual airport row pins that airport as a visible dot — SimFly-owned airports appear in their normal category color; non-owned airports appear as a gray dot. Each pinned or filtered result appears as a chip in the filter status strip with a ✕ to clear it. After every search action the map zooms to fit every currently-visible airport — typing KSEA then +KPDX frames both pinned airports together; adding more pins or filters expands the view as needed.

Additive search (“+” prefix) — prefix any search with + to accumulate results instead of replacing the current filter. Example: search Europe to filter to SimFly airports in Europe; then +Kyndra to also show Kyndra’s airports; then +California to also include California airports; then +EGLL to also pin EGLL as a gray dot. A non-prefixed search clears all owner, country, region, continent, and pinned filters and replaces them with the new result.

Negation search (“−” prefix) — prefix any search with − to exclude rather than include. −Germany hides all SimFly airports in Germany; −California hides all California airports; −Europe hides all European airports; −Kyndra removes Kyndra from the active owner filter or adds her to the excluded-owners set; −EGLL removes EGLL from pinned or adds it to the excluded ICAOs set. Excluded items appear as red-tinted chips in the filter status strip with a ✕ to un-exclude.

METAR on route assignment — clicking an airport dot to set the route departure or arrival automatically fetches that airport's current METAR (via the CheckWX API, with the same nearest-station fallback as the bulk ⛅ Get Weather button). When the data arrives, the airport's dot tooltip on the region map gains a compact weather row beneath the existing scenery / developer rows: a colored VFR / MVFR / IFR / LIFR pill, wind in DDD°/KKkt form, and visibility. Cached METARs (30-minute TTL) are reused, so re-selecting the same airport doesn't repeat the API call.

Route ICAO chips — when a departure and arrival airport are selected via dot-click routing, their ICAOs are shown in the header. Hover either chip to see the full airport tooltip: name, owner, category, elevation, runway details, and weather (if fetched). Owned airports remain clickable SimFly detail links; non-owned airports show the tooltip but no link. The ⇄ button between the two chips swaps departure and arrival in one click — same handler updates the route line, matrix highlight, and main-screen ICAO inputs.

Measurement — click 📐 Measurement in the header to activate the ruler. Click and drag anywhere on the map to draw a radius spoke and range circle; the label shows distance, true bearing, and magnetic bearing (sourced from the NOAA WMM API). Distance units adapt to scale: at 20,000 ft and under the label shows feet and meters; above that threshold it shows nm and km (2 decimal places up to 100 nm, whole numbers above). Release to freeze the measurement — it stays visible until explicitly cleared. Draw additional measurements; all stack simultaneously. Saved measurements are movable and resizable: grab the origin pin (center handle) to translate the whole measurement — direction and distance are preserved; grab the edge handle (the resize grip at the line's endpoint) to both resize and rotate the measurement — distance and bearing follow the cursor together. Each measurement also has a ✕ close handle at the top of the ring (12 o'clock, bearing 0°) — clicking it removes that specific measurement without disturbing any others. Cursor changes to a grab hand over the center, a resize arrow over the edge handle, and a pointer over the close handle. The on-map ✕ close handle is the per-measurement removal control; ✕ Clear All appears in the header whenever one or more measurements are on the map and wipes them all in one click. The distance / bearing label sits centered on the line for normal-length measurements, but auto-shifts below the line for short ones so the origin pin and edge handle stay fully visible. Starting a new region selection (⊙ Select Regions) automatically toggles measurement mode off so the two workflows don't compete for left-click — saved measurements stay on the map.

Right-mouse drag the map to pan, in any phase or mode. Left-click is always owned by whatever workflow is active (region draw, measurement draw, dot click, handle drag); right-mouse drag is the universal pan affordance so you can navigate the map without exiting what you're doing. A pure right-click without dragging preserves its pre-existing meaning — opens the Quick-Rate popover over an airport dot in explore / results phases, and cancels the in-progress region selection during region drawing.

Use ↩ Undo to step back one phase (removes the last region drawn). Click ⊙ DONE (results phase only) to collapse the distance matrix and return to free map exploration — region circles stay on the map so you can still reference them. Click ⊙ Select Regions to start a fresh selection (this clears any previous circles). Use ✕ Close to exit Region Mode entirely. The main-screen ✕ Reset button also closes Region Mode and clears all region state.
⬤
Scenery Library
The ⬤ Scenery split button in the map header controls your personal scenery library — a list of airports you have high-detail scenery for. Click the left area to cycle display modes: Highlight (black dot on each scenery airport), Only (filter the map to scenery airports only), and off. Click the ✏ icon on the right to open the library panel.

Adding airports from the map (fastest): Shift+click any airport dot at any time to instantly toggle it in or out of your library — no panel needed. Or click 📍 Add from map in the library footer to enter paint mode, where every dot click toggles scenery. A blue banner confirms the mode; click ✕ Done or press Escape to exit.

Folder scan (Chrome/Edge only): click 📂 Scan folder, point at your MSFS Community or X-Plane Custom Scenery folder, and the app detects airport codes from add-on subfolder names. A checkbox preview lets you confirm before importing.

Scenery config import (⋯ menu): import an X-Plane scenery_packs.ini or MSFS/P3D scenery.cfg directly — same checkbox preview as the folder scan. You can also drag and drop any of these files (or a .json backup) straight onto the modal.

FS Addon Compare import (⋯ menu): select a .har file captured from the FS Addon Compare website. Open FS Addon Compare → My Library in Chrome/Edge, open DevTools (F12) → Network tab, reload the page, then export and save as HAR.

Star ratings — every airport in the library has a 0–5 star rating shown next to its name. Click the left half of a star for a half rating, the right half for a full rating; click the same half again to clear back to zero. Ratings are saved automatically and round-trip through Export JSON / Import JSON. When a rating is set, it is also displayed in the airport’s tooltip on the region map.

Rate from the map — right-click any airport dot on the region map (or long-press for ~½ second on touch devices) to open a Quick-Rate popover anchored to that dot. Click stars to set the rating without leaving the map. If the airport is not yet in your Scenery Library, setting a rating adds it automatically (and turns on the Highlight overlay so the black scenery dot appears right away). Close the popover with ×, Esc, by clicking elsewhere on the map, or by zooming.

Sort and filter — the Show dropdown filters the list to All airports, SimFly-owned only (airports still owned by an active SimFly player), Rated only, or Unrated only. The Sort dropdown orders the list by ICAO, rating (high to low), or airport name. The text box above also searches by ICAO, addon name, and full airport name.

Store and Developer attribution — each library row has two inline text fields beneath the ICAO line: Store (the marketplace where the scenery was purchased) and Dev (the publisher / studio). Both fields autocomplete from a built-in list of common marketplaces and developers plus any custom values you've already typed on other rows, so your own conventions become suggestions over time. Both fields are persisted to local storage and round-trip through Export / Import JSON.

Developer filter — the new Developer dropdown next to Show / Sort narrows the modal list to a single developer. Selecting a developer also filters the region map: library airports whose developer doesn't match are hidden, airports not in the library are unaffected. An active filter shows as a Developer: <name> chip in the region-map filter strip with a ✕ to clear it.

🛠 Manage Values modal — click the 🛠 Manage Values button in the Scenery Library footer to open a bulk-edit view of every Store and Developer value in your library. Each value shows the number of airports using it. Edit a value and click Save to rename it on every affected airport (rename to the exact spelling of an existing value to merge duplicates — handy for fixing typos like iniBilds → iniBuilds, or normalising spacing like Fly Tampa → FlyTampa). The ✕ button clears the value from every airport that uses it, after a confirm prompt that reports exactly how many airports will be affected.

⚙ Developers modal — click the ⚙ Developers button in the Scenery Library footer to open a per-developer summary of your library. The modal lists every developer for whom you have at least one rated airport and shows: total airports, rated count, and the average star rating over rated entries (displayed at two-decimal precision, e.g. 3.75/5, with a proportionally-filled star widget). Unrated airports are excluded from the average so they don't pull the developer's rating down. Click any column header to sort. Click a developer row to expand or collapse their airport list; click the 🔍 button next to a developer's name to filter the Scenery Library to that developer.

The library is stored in your browser’s localStorage. Closing the panel after making changes prompts you to 💾 Download & Close to save a backup. Use Export JSON (⋯ menu) to back up or transfer to another machine at any time.
⭐
Scenery Star Rating Filter
The ⭐ Rating button in the map header opens the Scenery Rating filter panel. Hover over the five stars to preview a threshold, then click to set it — airports in the Scenery Library with a rating below that value are hidden on the map and removed from the distance matrix. Click the same star again to clear the filter.

The filter only applies to airports that are in your Scenery Library with a rating set — airports with no rating entry are always hidden when any threshold is active. If the Pilot Payout filter is also open, the Scenery Rating panel sits directly beneath it. Airport ratings are set via right-click (or long-press on touch) on any map dot.
💰
Pilot Payout Map
The 💰 Payout button in the Region Mode header switches the map to payout-percentage coloring. The scale is anchored so 50% = yellow and 60% = green, making those two values immediately distinguishable at a glance. Below that: 1–20% runs red through orange-red (the non-premium range, capped at 20%), 20–40% runs orange through amber, and 40–50% transitions from amber into yellow. Grey dots indicate airports with no payout data available.

A legend in the lower-left corner of the map shows the gradient scale while the mode is active. Click the button again to return to standard category/owner coloring.

Quick toggle for 0% airports — click the gray dot on the legend to switch the filter between 0% (show every airport) and 1% (hide airports with no payout set). When the filter is active the dot dims and gains a yellow inset ring as a visible state cue. The slider can still be used for any other threshold above 1%.

Payout data is imported via the SimFly KML Generator (v2.20.3+) using the Pilot Payout Data panel. The KML Generator stores it in your browser’s localStorage under the key simfly_payout_db, which this app reads automatically on load. Payout percentages are also shown in airport tooltips (after the owner name) whenever data is available.
🔖
Filter Status Strip
The row of chips between the 📐 Measurement button and the category circles gives you an at-a-glance summary of every active filter on the region map.

Display Mode — always visible; shows Category Colors or Payout Colors depending on the current color mode. Click the chip to toggle between the two modes.

Category Filtering — appears when at least one of the seven map categories is hidden. Click × to restore all categories.

N Owners Displayed — appears when the Airport Owners filter is active and shows how many owners’ airports are currently visible. Click × to show all owners.

Pilot Payout ≥ N% — appears when Payout mode is on and a minimum payout threshold is set above 0%. Click × to clear the threshold (the mode stays on).

Rating ≥ N stars — appears when the Scenery Rating filter is active with a threshold above zero. Click × to turn the filter off entirely.

The strip truncates automatically if it would overlap the category circles, always leaving at least 30 px of clearance.
📋  Release Notes
Show:
No releases match the selected filters.
v2.61.19 May 19, 2026 · Current Improved
  • Category clusters now sit directly above their filter buttons — the Dep / Arr category circle groups were centered on the full left/right halves of the block, but the filter buttons (capped narrower) sat slightly inboard of those half-centers, so the two rows didn't line up. The category row now uses the same geometry as the button row — matching gap and per-group max-width — so each cluster centers directly above its button. The thin vertical divider between the two groups was removed; it was redundant now that the groups are clearly separated and individually labeled.
v2.61.18 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Filter buttons centered as an equal-width pair — reverted the v2.61.17 per-half positioning. The Filter airports unsuitable… and Hide filtered airports buttons are once again centered together as a pair, and both now render at the same width — previously Hide filtered airports sized to its shorter label and looked noticeably narrower than its neighbour.
v2.61.17 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Filter buttons centered beneath their category clusters — the Filter airports unsuitable… and Hide filtered airports buttons were centered together as a pair in the middle of the row. Each button now sits in its own half of the filter block, centered within that half — so they line up neatly beneath the Dep and Arr category clusters above them. (When no aircraft is selected, only Hide filtered airports shows, centered in the right half beneath the Arr cluster.)
v2.61.16 May 19, 2026 Fix
  • Dep / Arr category clusters centered on their half of the screen — v2.61.15 spread the two category groups with space-between, which pushed them hard against the left and right margins on wide displays. Each group now takes an equal half of the filter block and centers its own contents, so the Dep cluster sits centered in the left half and the Arr cluster centered in the right half.
v2.61.15 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Distance filter block scales with the wider layout — after the v2.61.13 main-screen widening, the Distance slider, the Dep / Arr category filters, and the two filter buttons stayed at their old narrow widths, leaving them clustered in the middle of the screen. All three rows now share a common 1280px block width (filling 100% on narrower screens, capping on 4K): the distance slider widened from its fixed 750 px cap, the Dep and Arr category groups now spread to the block edges instead of huddling around the center separator, and the two filter buttons flex-grow to share the row width.
  • Help — Category Filter section corrected — the section still said the category circles sit “below the sort pills” (a control removed long ago) and described a single filter group. It now correctly says the circles sit below the distance slider, documents the independent Dep and Arr groups, and uses the real button labels (Inv, Hide filtered airports).
v2.61.14 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Help and Release Notes modals scale with the monitor — following the main-screen change in v2.61.13, the Help (fixed 680 px) and Release Notes (fixed 640 px) modals were also stranded narrow on 1440p and 4K displays. Their widths are now clamp(680px, 35vw, 1400px) and clamp(640px, 33vw, 1400px) respectively: pinned at the original width for any viewport ≈1920 px or narrower, scaling up to roughly a third of the viewport on bigger screens (≈1344 px / ≈1267 px at 4K), and capped at 1400 px so long help text never stretches into unreadably wide lines. The 1080p appearance is unchanged.
v2.61.13 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Main screen scales with the monitor on large displays — the main content column was hard-capped at 960 px, so it filled only half of a 1080p screen and looked stranded — barely a quarter — on 1440p and 4K monitors. The cap is now max(960px, 50vw): it tracks roughly 50% of the viewport width on big screens (≈1280 px at 1440p, ≈1920 px at 4K) while staying pinned at the original 960 px for any viewport 1920 px or narrower. At exactly 1920 px the two values are equal, so the 1080p layout is completely unchanged.
v2.61.12 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Main distance matrix pivot ⊙ now keeps the clicked airport under your mouse — same fix as v2.61.10 / v2.61.11 for the region matrix, applied to the owner distance matrix. Alternating between a departure pivot and an arrival pivot reorders both axes (new pivot axis sorts by distance; previous axis reverts to alpha), so the row or column you just clicked could jump out of view. _distPivotClick now captures the clicked header’s viewport rect before renderDistMatrix, then offsets distMatrixWrap.scrollTop or scrollLeft by the post-render delta so the same airport lands back under the cursor. Layered on top of the existing scroll-offset preservation that already prevented the blank-flash at the top of the table during re-sort.
v2.61.11 May 19, 2026 Fix
  • Pivot ⊙ scroll preservation now actually works — v2.61.10 targeted the wrong DOM element: it read/wrote scrollTop / scrollLeft on #rgMatrixWrap, but that container has overflow:clip and never scrolls. The real scroll viewport is its child #rgMatrixScroll (overflow:auto). The fix now writes to the correct element, so the clicked row or column header reliably stays under the cursor when alternating between dep and arr pivots.
v2.61.10 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Pivot ⊙ click keeps the clicked airport under your mouse — on the region matrix, alternating between sorting by a departure airport and sorting by an arrival airport reorders both axes (the new pivot axis sorts by distance; the previously-sorted axis returns to alpha). That meant the row or column you just clicked could jump several screens away, forcing you to scroll to find it. The matrix now captures the clicked header’s viewport position before re-rendering and adjusts scrollTop (for a dep pivot) or scrollLeft (for an arr pivot) so the same header lands back under the cursor. Lets you chain pivot clicks rapidly without losing your place.
  • Help panel — “Region Mode” section renamed to “Map” — the section title and the two in-body references to “the Region Mode header” (in the Scenery Library and Scenery Star Rating Filter sections) now read “the map header”, matching the ⊙ Map button that opens the panel. Two modal button tooltips (✕ Close and Send to Flight Planner →) updated to match — they previously read “Exit / Close Region Mode and …”.
v2.61.9 May 19, 2026 Fix
  • Spelling — “Furthest” → “Farthest” in the region matrix Sort dropdown — when a ⊙ pivot was active on an arrival column, the Sort dropdown showed e.g. Furthest KSEA while the parallel header tooltip already read Farthest dep first. Farther / farthest is the correct form for physical distance; the dropdown now matches the tooltip wording.
v2.61.8 May 19, 2026 Fix
  • Weather section no longer duplicated in route map info cards — when a route was highlighted and a METAR was cached for either endpoint, the Departure / Arrival info cards beneath the route map rendered the full decoded weather block twice: once via the shared airport tooltip builder (which already appends a Weather section when cache data is present) and again via a separate weather-append helper local to the info-card render. The redundant helper has been removed; cards now show exactly one Weather section per airport, matching the badge-hover tooltip layout.
  • Help panel — Row & Column Sorting rewritten for the ⊙ pivot system — the help section still described the legacy two-click sort pills + padlock UI that was replaced in v2.46.x by the one-click ⊙ pivot dots on each row and column header. The section now correctly explains: click the ⊙ dot on any header to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport; clicks cycle ⊙ → ▲ Nearest → ▼ Farthest → ⊙; row / column highlighting is independent of sorting. Hover tooltip example updated to match the live pivot-dot tooltips.
v2.61.7 May 19, 2026 Improved
  • Region matrix auto-pans map to show both circles — when the matrix panel first opens, the map now fits its view to encompass both the departure and arrival circles, with each pair filling roughly 80% of the visible map area. Uses Leaflet’s native circle bounds rather than a manual NM approximation, so the zoom level is always exact regardless of circle size or latitude.
v2.61.6 May 18, 2026 Improved
  • Pilot Payout filter survives color-mode breadcrumb switches — the Pilot Payout ≥ N% filter previously reset to 0 the moment payout color mode turned off, including when the user cycled the breadcrumb chip Payout Colors → Owner Colors or → Category Colors. The filter is now decoupled from the color mode: setting a threshold continues to hide airports below it on the map, in the matrix, and in the dep / arr highlight layer regardless of whether dots are coloured by category, payout, or owner. The Pilot Payout ≥ N% breadcrumb chip also stays visible across the switch so the filter can be cleared from any mode via the chip's ✕. The explicit 💰 Payout button still resets the filter when it turns the mode off — that path remains an explicit “reset payout” gesture.
v2.61.5 May 18, 2026 Improved
  • Hero line dims and hides its distance label while a route is active — the dep→arr centre-to-centre hero line (with its N nm midpoint chip) was visually competing with the selected route line and its ICAO → ICAO · N nm info chip whenever both were on the map at once. The hero line now drops to 50% opacity and its midpoint distance label is hidden as soon as a route is drawn between any dep / arr pair, then returns to full opacity with its label restored the moment the route is cleared (via the × Clear button, clicking the current departure, or any other clear path). Applies in every phase that shows the hero line.
v2.61.4 May 18, 2026 Fix
  • Freshly-pushed gist edits from KML Generator now surface on the next AA load — the SimFly_Airport_Overrides.json and SimFly_Airport_Categories.csv Gist fetches were missing the ?t=Date.now() cache-buster that the aircraft and payout Gist fetches already had, so GitHub's raw.githubusercontent.com CDN (which caches for several minutes) would keep serving stale content even after a hard refresh. Result: a continent / category / coordinate correction pushed from KML Generator could take 5–10 minutes to appear in Active Airports' region map filters, tooltips, and search. Both fetches now cache-bust like their siblings, matching how KML Generator already cache-busts every Gist read on its own side.
v2.61.3 May 18, 2026 Feature
  • Region-aware route selection while the matrix is visible — once both dep and arr regions are drawn, clicking any airport inside the dep region now sets or changes the departure, and clicking inside the arr region sets or changes the arrival, regardless of click order. Background-dot clicks (outside both regions) and explore-phase clicks still follow the existing “first click sets dep, next click sets arr” rolling chain. Overlap airports (inside both circles) toggle to whichever slot they aren’t already in. Region-mode clicks no longer cancel the route just because you clicked on the current departure — use the ✕ Clear route button for that.
  • Unique Arrivals region matrix mode — new toggle in the results bar next to Max per region. When active, the matrix keeps every departure row but hides any arrival column whose airport also appears in the departure region — trimming duplicate arrivals so the matrix shows only routes from any dep airport to an airport unique to the arr region. Map dots are untouched: every airport in either region stays visible (still useful for routing via dot clicks). The Venn-icon button uses an asymmetric design — dep circle outlined, arr-minus-dep crescent filled in arrival orange when active — so the “subtract dep from arr” effect reads at a glance. Below 1500 px the “Unique Arrivals” text label collapses to icon-only with the tooltip preserved.
  • Unique Arrivals cycles arr-cap direction (Off → Far → Near → Off) — the toggle is now a three-state cycle. While the mode is active, Max per region applies asymmetrically: the dep cap always keeps the N airports closest to the dep center, but the arr cap can be set to keep the N airports farthest from arr center (default on activation — least likely to overlap dep) or nearest to arr center (second click). The active direction is shown directly on the button as ↗ Far or ↘ Near, and the button tooltip explains both the current state and what the next click will do. The cap-selector hover tooltip updates in sync so hovering it also reveals the active direction.
v2.60.5 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Region matrix row/column selection no longer clears after weather fetch — clicking a matrix cell and then having the weather auto-fetch complete for that route was causing the row/column highlight to disappear (alternating pattern: works → clears → works → clears). Root cause: _wxFetchOne called _rgApplyMapFilter() on completion, which re-renders the full matrix in the results phase and wipes the active selection. Fixed by replacing that call with _rgRenderDots() + _rgHighlightDots() directly — weather data only needs the dot tooltip cache and dot layer refreshed, not a full matrix rebuild.
v2.60.4 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Footer "SimFly Active Player-Owned Airports" converted to static text — the footer entry was previously a hyperlink to a Google Earth KML view that is no longer maintained. It is now plain text so there is no broken destination to click.
v2.60.3 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Category filter button tooltips updated — hovering a numbered category circle (1–7) now shows an airport-type description instead of an aircraft description. Categories now read: 1 = Small unpaved airstrips and local grass fields; 2 = Minor rural airfields and flight school bases; 3 = Small regional and small domestic airports; 4 = Mid-sized domestic and regional commercial hubs; 5 = Large domestic and standard international facilities; 6 = Major international hubs with high traffic volume; 7 = Mega-hubs and world-famous mega-airports. Applies to all filter rows (Distance Matrix dep/arr, Region Mode map filter, Region Mode results bar dep/arr).
v2.60.2 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Map opened from ?pilots= handoff now defaults to owner colors — when Active Airports is launched from the Recip Tracker / Relationship Manager Lite 🗺 Send to AA → button (which carries the selected pilots in the ?pilots= URL parameter), the map now opens with owner-color rendering enabled by default so each pilot's airports are immediately distinguishable. Category and payout color modes are still reachable via the 🎨 button or the mode cycle. Payout mode is force-cleared on this code path so the two modes don't fight on first render. The bare ⊚ Map button (no URL handoff) is unchanged — it still opens in category-color mode.
v2.60.1 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Main-screen ⊚ Regions button renamed to ⊚ Map — the button now reads ⊚ Map to better reflect that the panel is an interactive map with airport dots, search, filters, and measurement tools — not just a region-drawing workflow. The cross-region distance matrix is still the same one click deeper (the in-map ⊚ Select Regions button). Help text updated to match.
v2.60.0 May 17, 2026 Feature
  • Search by scenery developer in the region-map search box — type a developer name (e.g. Orbx, FlyTampa, iniBuilds) to see matching developers in the dropdown alongside owner / country / region / continent / airport results. Click Filter map → to filter the map dots to airports with that developer set in your Scenery Library. The same prefix semantics as every other filter type apply: +Orbx adds Orbx alongside whatever's already filtered, −Orbx excludes Orbx airports specifically. A 📦 icon distinguishes developer results from country / region / continent results in the dropdown.
  • “N Developers” breadcrumb chip with rich hover legend — an active developer filter adds a chip to the filter status strip showing either the single developer name or N Developers for multi-developer filters. Hovering the chip opens a popover listing every active developer with a right-justified count of currently-visible airports for each one. The count honors every other active map filter (category, country, region, continent, payout, star, scenery, owner, exclusion sets) so it reflects exactly what's rendered on the map — toggle off a category and the count drops live the next time you hover. Excluded developers render as red −Developer chips alongside the other exclusion chips.
v2.59.7 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Owner color legend shows live per-owner airport counts — hovering the Airport Owners dropdown or the N Owners Displayed breadcrumb chip now shows a right-justified count of currently-visible airports next to each owner name in the popover. The count is recomputed from scratch on every hover using the same visibility chokepoint that decides dot rendering, so it tracks every other map-wide filter exactly — toggling off a category, applying a country / region / continent filter, or raising the payout / star threshold all immediately reflect in the legend the next time you hover.
v2.59.6 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Final orphan classified — WX53 (Bugalaga Airstrip, Central Papua, Indonesia) added to the manual ICAO override table with country ID, region ID-PT, continent Asia. Combined with v2.59.5's LL48 entry, this means every SimFly-owned airport in the fleet now resolves cleanly to country and continent codes — no more leakage through any −Country / −Region / −Continent filter.
v2.59.5 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Manual ICAO overrides for airports the prefix table can't resolve — the v2.59.4 sweep classified 19 of 21 orphan airports via ICAO 2-letter prefix. The two stragglers (WX53 and LL48) use prefixes that are either non-standard or ambiguous — LL is officially Israel per ICAO Annex 10, so the prefix table can't safely claim it for a US airport. New per-ICAO override table (_RG_ICAO_MANUAL_OVERRIDES) handles these one-offs: LL48 (Wilcoxon Airport) is now pinned to US / US-IL / North America for all three geo filters. Region (state / province) filtering also gains a dedicated resolver (_rgRecRegion) that consults the override table; previously the region filter could only fall through to rec.iso_region with no fallback.
v2.59.4 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Country filters now catch ICAO-prefix-only airports too — v2.59.3 added an ICAO 2-letter prefix fallback for the continent resolver, fixing −Europe. The same orphan airports (no iso_country in their record) were still slipping through country filters like −Italy or −United States. This release adds the complementary ICAO prefix → ISO country fallback and routes every country-filter check through it — the chokepoint helper, the in-region highlight loops, the pinned-airports block, and both the country and continent search-result counters. 19 of 21 previously-orphan airports now classify correctly (LP** → PT, LI** → IT, LE** → ES, EG** → GB, etc.); the remaining 2 (WX53 Indonesia, LL48 US) use non-standard prefixes outside ICAO Annex 10. Verified in browser: −Italy filter now removes all 85 Italian airports (including the 7 that lacked iso_country); −United States removes all 206. Search-result counts in the dropdown now equal the exact set that will actually be filtered.
v2.59.3 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Continent filter now catches airports missing country metadata — a small number of SimFly airports (e.g. LPBE, LIBZ, LEDT, EGON — ~13 European, ~21 total across all continents) are added directly by SimFly without country or continent metadata; their record carries only ICAO, name, and lat / lng. Continent filters like −Europe couldn't classify them, so they slipped through and stayed visible on the map. The continent resolver now falls back to a comprehensive ICAO 2-letter prefix table when both continent and iso_country are missing — LP**, LI**, LE**, EG**, etc. all map to EU, plus full coverage of NA / SA / AF / AS / OC / AN prefixes — so these orphan airports are now properly classifiable.
v2.59.2 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Region map dot tooltips work again after drawing region circles — the v2.56.0 canvas-renderer overhaul added a second canvas element to the map's overlay pane for measurement / region / range geometry. Because both that canvas and the airport-dot canvas defaulted to the same pane with pointer-events: auto, the geometry canvas sat on top of the dot canvas and silently captured every mousemove as soon as the dep / arr region rings rendered — so dot mouseover never fired and tooltips stopped appearing on hover. The geometry canvas now lives in its own dedicated pane (z-index 420, between the dot pane and the route-line pane) with pointer-events: none, so mouse events fall through to the dot canvas below it as before.
v2.59.1 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Region map dot tooltips no longer stay disabled after closing mid-draw — clicking ⊚ Select Regions puts the map in a drawing phase that intentionally suppresses dot tooltips so the cursor stays focused on placing the region. If the modal was then closed (via the × close button) without completing the draw or pressing Cancel, the drawing phase persisted across the close, and reopening the modal silently kept tooltips disabled because the map-init path short-circuits when the map already exists. The close handler now resets transient drawing phases back to explore on the way out; completed-region state (results phase) still survives a close / reopen cycle as before.
v2.59.0 May 17, 2026 Fix
  • Region matrix now honors every map-wide filter — previously the matrix only respected the map-wide owner filter. Filtering the map to a country, region, continent, pinned set, exclusion list, scenery / developer toggle, global category, payout, or star rating — then drawing region circles — would still populate the matrix with airports that had been hidden on the map. The matrix and dot rendering now share a single chokepoint that gates visibility against all those filters, so the matrix can never show an airport the map has hidden. Concretely: filter to Australia, draw region circles in the USA → the matrix is empty (was: matrix showed USA airports despite no USA dots on the map).
  • Live re-filtering as you change map filters with regions drawn — every filter mutation site (search filters, exclude chips, global category buttons, payout slider, star filter, scenery toggle) now routes through the same shared "filter changed" applier. Adjusting any map-wide filter while in results phase immediately re-filters the matrix in addition to re-rendering dots; previously only the owner filter triggered a matrix refresh.
v2.58.0 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Right-mouse drag pans the map throughout region mode — v2.57.0 introduced right-mouse pan only while measurement mode was active. It now applies universally in region mode: every phase (explore, region drawing, results) and every mode honors right-drag for panning. Left-click stays owned by phase-specific workflows (region draw, measurement draw, dot click, handle drag), so right-click is the consistent way to navigate the map without exiting whatever workflow is active. The browser context menu is suppressed across the entire region map.
  • Pre-existing right-click gestures preserved for the no-drag case — right-clicking an airport dot in explore / results phases still opens the Quick-Rate popover; right-clicking the map background during region drawing still cancels the in-progress selection. Both gestures fire only on a pure right-click with no cursor movement; the moment you drag, the gesture is treated as a pan and the popover / cancel is suppressed.
v2.57.0 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Main-screen airport tooltip now includes weather — hovering the big departure or arrival ICAO/name text in the main app screen now appends a METAR section to the tooltip (flight category pill, raw text, wind, visibility, sky, temp/dew, altimeter, observed time) — same rich format already used by the region-map dot tooltips. Weather is pulled from the existing CheckWX auto-fetch that runs on every route change, so no new network traffic. If you hover before the METAR arrives, the tooltip refreshes itself in place the moment the data lands.
  • Right-mouse-drag pans the map in measurement mode — left-click is reserved for drawing and dragging measurements, so right-click is now the explicit pan affordance while measurement mode is on. The browser context menu is suppressed while measurement mode is active so it doesn't pop up mid-pan. The cursor flips to grabbing while right-dragging, then returns to the crosshair on release.
  • Entering region-draw mode auto-deactivates measurement mode — clicking ⊚ Select Regions with measurement mode active used to leave both modes competing for left-click. The region-draw button now toggles measurement off before starting the region workflow. Saved measurements stay on the map — only the drawing mode is flipped off.
  • Close × click no longer pans the map — if your cursor moved a few pixels between mousedown and mouseup on a measurement's × close handle, the map could start panning behind the dismissal. The click now disables map dragging for the duration of the gesture so the measurement just vanishes cleanly.
  • Fix: center-dragging a short measurement no longer permanently centers its label — when a saved measurement was short enough that its distance/bearing label had been auto-shifted below the line (so the endpoints stayed visible), grabbing the origin pin and translating the measurement would snap the label back onto the line, hiding the endpoints. The label now correctly re-applies its below-the-line offset on release.
  • Measurement-handle DOM rebuild reduced — each frame of a drag was calling setIcon on the edge and close handles, rebuilding their inner DOM elements unnecessarily. Handles now cache their last-applied color and only run setIcon when the tile mode actually flips (purple ↔ yellow); routine drags use just setLatLng.
v2.56.1 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • × Clear All visible with just one measurement — previously the bulk-clear button only appeared once two or more measurements were on the map (the on-map × close handle was assumed sufficient for the single-measurement case). It now appears as soon as a single measurement exists, so the "wipe everything" entry point is always available regardless of count.
v2.56.0 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Region map performance fix — canvas renderer for drawn geometry — previously the measurement rings, region dep / arr circles, and aircraft range ring all used Leaflet's default SVG renderer. At high zoom, a saved measurement drawn at a wider view ballooned into a multi-thousand-pixel-circumference dashed SVG path, pushing the renderer's bounding box past the browser compositor's max-texture limit and forcing CPU rasterization on every pan, zoom, or even mouse move — the more saved measurements you had, the worse it got. All of this drawn geometry now uses a dedicated canvas renderer (separate from the airport-dot canvas, so the two don't trigger each other). Canvas keeps the drawing surface viewport-sized regardless of underlying geometry, so general map performance no longer degrades as saved measurements pile up.
  • Measurement label DOM no longer rebuilt every frame during draw — the live-drawing path used to construct a fresh L.divIcon and call setIcon on the label marker on every animation frame, which tore down and rebuilt the inner DOM element each time even though only the distance / bearing text actually changed. The label's inner wrapper is now cached on first creation and patched in place via innerHTML, so the per-frame work is a single text update instead of a full DOM swap.
  • Saved-measurement center-drag stops forcing layouts — translating a whole saved measurement (grab the origin pin) was calling the label-occlusion fit routine on every frame for the dragged entry. Since translation doesn't change the line's shape, the fit result was identical each time; the only effect was an unnecessary synchronous layout per frame. The fit routine is now skipped during center drag, and runs exactly once on mouse release.
v2.55.0 May 17, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement Undo button removed — the standalone ↩ Undo button next to the Measurement control has been retired. Each saved measurement already carries its own on-map × close handle (at the 12 o'clock position) for removing that specific entry, which is more precise than a "pop the newest" undo. × Clear All remains available when two or more measurements are on the map.
  • Range / bearing label auto-shifts to avoid covering the endpoints — on short measurements the midpoint-anchored chip could extend past one or both endpoints and obscure the begin / end markers (origin pin and edge handle). The label now detects when its bounding box would overrun an endpoint and slides itself below the line by just enough to clear it — the line, pin, and edge handle stay fully visible. Longer lines keep the centered look unchanged.
v2.53.3 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement label rides the endpoint during drag, snaps to center on release — centering the distance/bearing chip on the line made it hard to see the cursor target when drawing or adjusting an east/west line. The label now anchors at the line's endpoint with an 18 px below-line offset while the mouse is held down, so the chip never sits on top of the spot you're aiming at. The moment you release the mouse, the label re-anchors to the midpoint of the line (centered as in v2.53.1). Behavior applies to both the initial draw and the saved-measurement edge-handle drag.
v2.53.2 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement edge handle hides during drag — while you're adjusting a saved measurement's range / bearing, the magenta resize handle now disappears so it doesn't obscure the line endpoint you're aiming at. The handle reappears the moment you release the mouse, snapped to the new endpoint.
v2.53.1 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement distance / bearing label now centered on the line — previously the chip sat at the geodesic 58% point with an extra 8 px nudge to the right of its anchor, which clearly biased the label toward the endpoint. Both the live-drawing and saved-redraw paths now anchor the label at the geometric midpoint of the line (50%) and use a translate(-50%, -50%) wrapper so the chip is centered on its anchor coordinate. The chip now sits visually centered along the line at any bearing.
v2.53.0 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement edge handle is now a rotate + resize control — previously the resize handle sat at a fixed 90° east point on the ring and dragging it kept the bearing locked. The edge handle now lives at the line's actual endpoint and dragging it rotates AND resizes the measurement at once: the line follows the cursor in both distance and direction. The ✕ close handle stays pinned at 12 o'clock (bearing 0° from origin) regardless of where you rotate the line to, so it's always in a predictable spot.
v2.52.1 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Measurement ✕ close handle moved to 12 o'clock — previously sat at 270° (9 o'clock / west) opposite the 90° east resize handle. Bearing changed to 0° so the close button now sits at the top of the ring, putting it well clear of dot clusters that tend to live east and west of populated regions. Hit-test bearing also updated to match.
v2.52.0 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Dep / Arr Owner dropdowns now honor the map-wide Airport Owners filter — if the Airport Owners header filter is restricting the world map to a subset of pilots, the Dep Owners and Arr Owners dropdowns inside the results bar now only offer those same pilots, even if the dep / arr region circles contain airports owned by additional pilots that the map is hiding. Picking from a list of owners you can’t see was confusing — the dropdown lists are now coherent with the dots on the map. The dropdowns refresh automatically when the map-wide filter changes; existing dep / arr selections are preserved through the rebuild.
v2.51.3 May 16, 2026 Fix
  • Owner-color legend popover dismissed when the chip × is clicked — clicking the ✕ on the N Owners Displayed filter chip removed the chip itself from the breadcrumb strip, but the legend popover that opened on hover stayed visible because the chip vanished from under the cursor before any mouseleave fired. The chip's clear-filter callback now explicitly calls _rgHideOwnerLegend() before tearing down the filter, so the popover always goes away with the chip.
v2.51.2 May 16, 2026 Fix
  • Owner-color legend popover no longer overlaps the open Airport Owners menu — the hover handler was attached to the entire .rg-owner-wrap parent, which also contains the dropdown panel. Opening the dropdown and moving the mouse into it kept the legend popover open on top, blocking access to the owner rows. The mouseenter / mouseleave handlers now live on the Airport Owners button and the 🎨 button themselves; the dropdown panel is no longer a hover target, so it stays unobstructed while open.
v2.51.1 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Region matrix text — heavier & darker in light mode — column/row headers and cell text were drawn at muted greys with the default 600 weight. In light mode they now use --text dark navy at 700 / 600 / 500 weights (headers / cells / owner sub-label) for noticeably stronger legibility against the cream background. Dark mode unchanged.
  • Step 1 / Step 2 phase instruction overlay legible in light mode — v2.51.0 painted the overlay dark blue with white text, which left the dep / arr STEP badges (dark blue / dark amber text inside an inner pill) low-contrast on the same dark background. Light mode now uses a cream overlay with dark text, plus light blue / light amber tints on the badge pills, so every element reads at high contrast. Dark mode unchanged.
  • Owner Colors filter chip count consistent with siblings — the chip now reads Owner Colors (N) where N is the number of airports currently visible on the map, matching the format of Category Colors (N) and Payout Colors (N). Previously it showed the count of selected owners, which was the odd one out.
  • Hovering the “N Owners Displayed” chip shows the rich color legend — the chip previously had a plain title-attribute tooltip with owner names + airport counts. It now opens the same color-swatch popover used when hovering the Airport Owners dropdown, anchored beneath the chip itself so the color key is one glance away from the filter strip.
  • Owner color legend popover now left-aligns under the Airport Owners button — the popover previously hung off the right edge of the wrap (which included the 🎨 button), so it sat to the right of the dropdown text. It now uses fixed positioning with JS-computed coordinates pinned to the dropdown button's left edge, with viewport clamping so it never spills off the right side of the screen.
v2.51.0 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Light-mode accessibility sweep — ~32 CSS rules across the region map and every modal had hardcoded dark backgrounds (rgba(0,8,18,0.X)), white text, or white-tinted borders that worked in dark mode but were illegible or invisible on the cream light-mode surface. A new consolidated light-mode accessibility overrides CSS block now patches every affected surface: breadcrumb filter chips, the phase-instruction overlay, region-draw resize / close handles, on-map labels (route info, dep→arr hero distance, “Crosses antimeridian” chip, measurement ring labels, Leaflet tooltips), airport dot tooltip with its arrow tip, all flight-category dot text colours inside it, weather details / nearest-station notes, the owner-stripe fallback, the owner-colour legend popover, the Pilot Payout legend and Scenery Rating legend (with their inline-styled close × buttons and faint "color:#aaa" muted labels), the Developers modal column-header hover state, and the Help modal’s inline code background. Inline-styled measurement ring labels are repainted via !important so the cream-backed pill wins over the dark-navy inline style. Dark mode is entirely unchanged — every affected surface keeps its original dark-mode computed colours.
v2.50.1 May 16, 2026 Fix
  • US spelling — “colour” → “color” throughout — corrected 107 occurrences of British “colour” (including “coloured”, “colours”, “color-coded”) across comments, release notes, help text, and user-visible strings (Owner Colors legend, 🎨 button tooltip, etc.) so the in-app text is consistently American English.
v2.50.0 May 16, 2026 Feature
  • Owner-colored map dots — whenever the Airport Owners filter is active you can now color every visible dot by its owner instead of by category or pilot payout. A new 🎨 button sits immediately to the right of the Airport Owners dropdown (greyed out when no filter is active, glowing rainbow when on). The Category Colors (n) / Payout Colors (n) chip in the filter strip extends to a three-way cycle when owner filtering is active: category → payout → owner → category. Up to 16 selected owners get a maximally-distinct curated color each; beyond 16 we fall back to the hash-based palette used by the region-matrix owner stripes.
  • Airport Owners hover legend — hovering the Airport Owners dropdown (or the new 🎨 button) reveals a small popover listing every owner currently in the filter with their assigned color swatch, so the color key is always one glance away.
  • Selected route: dep / arr are now interactive — in the matrix info bar the blue departure and orange arrival labels weren't clickable and didn't show tooltips. They now match the header chips: the same airport tooltip (name, owner, runways, weather) on hover, the same SimFly-details link on click for owned airports, and a colored weather flight-category dot (VFR / MVFR / IFR / LIFR) appears next to each name when a METAR has been fetched.
v2.49.2 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Avg Rating sort tiebreaker — number of rated airports — in the View Developer Stats modal, when two developers have the same average star rating, the developer with more rated airports now appears first. Previously the tie fell through to alphabetical, which obscured the more substantive comparison (a 4.0/5 average across 12 airports is a stronger signal than 4.0/5 across 2). Tiebreaker direction is fixed — more rated always wins, regardless of whether the user sorted Avg Rating ascending or descending. Final fallback is still alphabetical.
v2.49.1 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Flight-category dot on the region-map route chips — the two large ICAO chips in the Region Mode header (the KSEA → KPDX / KSEA ↔ KPDX display) now show a colored weather dot after each ICAO once a METAR is in the cache. Uses the same VFR/MVFR/IFR/LIFR palette as the matrix dots and the per-airport tooltip flight-category pill, so route weather reads at a glance without opening any tooltip. Combined with v2.49.0's auto-fetch on route assignment, the dot appears almost immediately whenever you set departure or arrival. The chip's existing hover tooltip continues to show the full decoded weather block (METAR text, wind, visibility, ceiling, etc.) — the dot is a non-interactive visual cue (pointer-events:none) that doesn't fight the chip's tooltip.
v2.49.0 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Auto-fetch weather for the route endpoints — the main UI now pulls a fresh METAR for the departure and arrival airports automatically whenever either ICAO is set, swapped, or older than 30 minutes. New helpers _wxAutoFetchRoute() + _wxAutoFetchRouteDebounced() wired into _calcDistance() so every route-change path triggers it (typing, ⇄ swap, URL params, Region-Mode handoff, owner-dropdown→ICAO fills). A 600 ms debounce keeps rapid keystrokes from firing per-character API calls. _wxFetchOne() short-circuits on a fresh cache entry, so this only hits the network when an endpoint is genuinely stale.
  • Auto-refresh on tab focus — visibilitychange and focus listeners re-run the auto-fetch the moment you switch back to the tab, so a route that's been on screen past the 30 min TTL gets fresh data the instant you look at it.
  • Tooltips, matrix dimming, and distance map refresh on new METAR — when an auto-fetch actually pulls new data, the matrix is re-rendered so every column/row tooltip reflects the new METAR, _distApplyFilter() re-evaluates the suitability dimming (DA changes can flip airports across the runway-length line), and _dmRenderMap() refreshes the open distance map info cards. The button-driven ⛅ Get Weather still works for fetching the full matrix in one batch.
v2.48.0 May 16, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft chip typography refresh — ICAO codes now render in Rajdhani (replaces Orbitron, whose D/O/0 collide on alphanumeric codes like C750 and BE24) at a larger size with tightened letter-spacing. Aircraft names and the per-chip range readout are larger and brighter so the chip grid scans more easily at a glance.
  • Yellow hover highlight on chip text — hovering a chip now turns all three text lines (ICAO, name, range) yellow for stronger visual feedback than the prior subtle muted→white shift.
  • Per-chip spec line simplified to range — the small spec readout inside each chip card now shows only ↔ X nm instead of TO X ft · LDG Y ft · ↔ Z nm. The full spec strip below the grid (which appears after selecting an aircraft) still shows takeoff and landing distances, so no data is lost — just decluttered at the grid level.
  • Aircraft database — 24 max-range corrections — audited every aircraft's Max_Range value in the source Gist against manufacturer specs and Wikipedia. Pushed corrections for HondaJet HA-420 (6,910 → 1,547 nm), MD-11 (2,510 → 6,725 nm), PC-12 (750 → 1,845 nm), E-190 (1,650 → 2,450 nm) and 20 others. The app picks the new values up automatically on next load.
v2.47.6 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Footer button renamed — the ⚙ Developers button in the Scenery Library footer is now ⚙ View Developer Stats, making it clearer that the action opens a read-only summary view (counts, average ratings) rather than an editing surface.
v2.47.5 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Footer button renamed — the Scenery Library footer button labelled 🛠 Manage Values is now 🛠 Manage Stores and Developers. Self-explanatory label that makes the modal's purpose clear at a glance.
v2.47.4 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Backup banner message simplified — the unsaved-changes notice now reads just "N unsaved change" / "N unsaved changes" instead of the wordier "N unsaved changes — download a backup before closing?". The trailing question is now redundant given the explicit 💾 Save and Close buttons next to it (each with its own descriptive tooltip from v2.47.3). Static placeholder shown before the count populates also trimmed to plain "Unsaved changes".
v2.47.3 May 15, 2026 Fix
  • Backup banner tooltips clarified — 💾 Save now reads "Save scenery database" on hover and Close reads "Close window without saving", replacing the longer carried-over hints from v2.47.2.
v2.47.2 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library backup banner buttons relabelled — the unsaved-changes banner now uses shorter, more accurate labels. 💾 Save (was "💾 Download & Close") downloads the JSON backup and dismisses the banner without closing the modal, so you can keep editing right after a snapshot. Close (was "Close anyway") closes the modal while preserving the unsaved-changes state — the banner reappears next time you open the modal until you actually export. Dirty-flag semantics are unchanged; only the labels and the no-longer-auto-close behaviour for Save are new.
v2.47.1 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Tab autocompletes the Store and Developer inputs — in the Scenery Library, typing simm in the Store field then pressing Tab now fills the topmost matching value from the dropdown (e.g. SimMarket); same for Developer (ini → iniBuilds). The match is case-insensitive and chooses the first alphabetical startsWith hit from the existing autocomplete list (presets plus values already used elsewhere in your library). Tab still advances focus normally afterwards, and the new value persists on blur via the existing onchange handler. Empty input, exact matches, no matches, Shift+Tab, and non-Tab keys all fall through silently so nothing else changes.
v2.47.0 May 15, 2026 Feature
  • Airport data overrides now consumed at load time — AA previously fetched SimFly_Airport_Overrides.json but only read its runwaySupplements bucket; the fieldOverrides (per-field corrections like region/city/IATA/coordinates merged on top of an OurAirports record) and overrides (full records for airports absent from OurAirports) buckets were ignored. Both are now merged into airportDB in a single one-shot pass right after the OurAirports CSV is parsed — field patches via Object.assign onto the existing record, full overrides as new entries keyed by ICAO. Result: every existing consumer (region map, header tooltips, search panels, owner dropdowns, region/country/state lookups) automatically reflects any augmentation entered through the KML Generator's Edit Airport tab. Zero runtime cost — the merge happens once at boot inside the same async block that's already parsing the OurAirports CSV; the region map's hot render path is untouched. Load-time console emits Airport overrides applied: N field patches, M new records when any are present.
v2.46.1 May 15, 2026 Fix
  • "Add to Recip Tracker" now reliably reaches RT — the SimBrief flow's Add to Recip Tracker action previously did a single gist PATCH and swallowed every failure silently, which meant: (a) you couldn't tell whether the save succeeded, and (b) if Recip Tracker was already open in another tab, RT's own autosave would race over the PATCH and overwrite the newly-added pilot back out of the gist. The flow now stamps each new pilot into localStorage['simfly_recip_inbox'] first — that path survives every gist failure mode (network, 401, race) and is drained by Recip Tracker v2.36.0+ on startup, on cross-tab storage events, and on visibility changes. Result: new pilots actually appear in the RT Balance Queue right after you click Add to Recip Tracker & File, no manual refresh required.
  • Toast feedback on the Recip Tracker save attempt — a non-blocking pill appears at the bottom-right of the AA window confirming the outcome: ✓ N pilots added to Recip Tracker on success, or Recip Tracker save failed (HTTP 401) — queued locally; N pilot(s) will sync next time RT runs on token/network failure. The toast auto-dismisses after 4 seconds. SimBrief still opens immediately either way; the toast doesn't block anything.
v2.46.0 May 15, 2026 Feature
  • New 🛠 Manage Values modal — bulk-edit the Store and Developer values used across your Scenery Library. Lists every distinct value in two side-by-side tables (one per field) with an airport count next to each. Edit a value and click Save to rename it on every airport that uses it — the workhorse for fixing typos (e.g. iniBilds → iniBuilds) and merging duplicates (rename one variant to the exact spelling of another and they fold together). The ✕ button clears the value from every airport that uses it; a confirm prompt with an explicit airport count fires before any destructive change. Launched from the Scenery Library footer (new 🛠 Manage Values button next to ⚙ Developers). All edits propagate live to the library list, the region-map dot tooltips, the Developer filter dropdown, the Developers modal, and the JSON export/import.
  • Scenery Library and Developers modals now use 75% of the viewport height — both modals were a bit cramped at their old fixed heights, especially with the larger row fonts. The modal container now has min-height:75vh; max-height:90vh so they always feel roomy. Inside the Library modal, the airport list itself grows to fill the available space (was capped at a fixed 240 px); the Developers table similarly takes the full body height now that the old 80vh cap is gone.
v2.45.6 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — rating cluster trimmed 25% — the v2.45.5 +75% bump on stars / rating score / ✕ delete was a touch too large; this release dials all four back by 25% (net +31% vs the original baseline). Stars 2.98 → 2.24 rem with padding 7px → 5px, rating number 1.61 → 1.21 rem with min-width 4.2 → 3.15 rem, ✕ button 1.38 → 1.04 rem with proportional padding. Click targets remain comfortably larger than the original but no longer dominate the row.
v2.45.5 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — rating stars, score, and ✕ button +75% — per-row interactive stars (1.7 → 2.98 rem), the numeric rating badge next to them (0.92 → 1.61 rem with a wider min-width so 5.0/5 reads cleanly), and the ✕ delete button (0.79 → 1.38 rem with proportional padding) are all 75% larger. Click targets are correspondingly bigger so half-star rating and row removal are easier to hit. The Quick-Rate popover keeps its own existing star size via the existing override.
v2.45.4 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Scenery Library — Store and Developer inputs +30% — the inline Store and Dev text fields on each row are now noticeably larger (font 0.85 → 1.11 rem, label font 0.78 → 1.01 rem, min-width 9 → 11.7 rem, max-width 14 → 18.2 rem, with proportional padding). Easier to scan and click, and the browser-rendered autocomplete popup inherits the bigger input font in most browsers, so the suggestion list reads larger too.
v2.45.3 May 15, 2026 Improved
  • Developers modal — bigger fonts, category-colored drill-down ICAOs, SimFly links — every text size in the modal is +40% (intro, table headers and body cells, sort indicators, average-rating number, drill-down list, empty-state text), and the static star widget in the rating column scales in proportion (1.05 → 1.47 rem) so the visual balance matches the larger surrounding text. Drill-down ICAOs no longer render as a comma-separated string — each ICAO is its own colored chip painted in the same SimFly category palette used elsewhere in the app, with a thin bordered pill background.
