  • Owner panel: arrow-key navigation and spacebar toggle — while a Dep Owners or Arr Owners panel is open, pressing ↓ moves a highlight down through the visible (filtered) rows; ↑ moves back up, and arrowing above the first row returns focus to the search box. Pressing Space toggles the highlighted row checked or unchecked. Space only acts as a toggle when arrow navigation is active — if no row is highlighted (the cursor is in the search box), Space types a space character as normal, allowing names with spaces to be searched correctly. Highlighting is preserved after each Space toggle so you can toggle, move, toggle without lifting your hands.
v2.21.13 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner panel: auto-focus filter as you type — while a Dep Owners or Arr Owners panel is open, any character you type is automatically routed to the Filter owners search box without requiring a manual click to focus it first. Backspace trims the last character; Enter clears the filter as before. Focus also returns to the search box after clicking a name row or pressing All / None, so the full type → select → Enter → type again workflow requires no extra clicks.
v2.21.12 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Crosshair cursor maintained throughout Measure Distance mode — the crosshair was set correctly when activating the tool, but mouse-move events in the results phase called _rgSetCursor on every tick, silently overriding it. The mouse-move handler now returns early whenever measure mode is active (not only while a drag is in progress), so the cursor stays as a crosshair for the full duration of the tool.
v2.21.11 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner search: ✕ clear button on the input — a small ✕ button appears at the right end of the Filter owners text box whenever search text is present. Clicking it clears the text and restores the full owner list, identical to pressing Enter, without leaving the keyboard.
v2.21.10 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Measure Distance: Undo and Clear All buttons — the ↩ Clear button is renamed ↩ Undo to better reflect that it removes only the last measurement. A new ✕ Clear All button appears to its right whenever two or more measurements are saved, and removes all of them at once. Both buttons auto-hide when there is nothing to act on.
v2.21.9 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Measure Distance tool: stuck-ring edge case fixed — a click with no drag (mousedown + immediate mouseup) left the origin point set. Subsequent mouse movement immediately drew a new measurement ring from that stale origin, and every further click repeated the cycle. Origin is now always cleared on mouseup, regardless of whether any dragging occurred. Additionally, calling Clear Live (e.g. toggling the tool off mid-drag) now re-enables map panning if dragging was disabled by the in-progress draw.
  • Done button collapses the matrix panel only — clicking ⊙ DONE now hides the distance matrix and results bar and returns to free map exploration, leaving the drawn region circles visible on the map. Previously it triggered a full reset, clearing the circles. Clicking ⊙ Select Regions still performs the full reset and clears old circles before a new selection begins.
  • Owner search All / None scoped to visible rows — while text is typed in the owner search box, the All and None buttons now apply only to the rows that match the search. Rows hidden by the filter keep their current checked/unchecked state. With no search text active, All and None continue to affect the complete list as before.
  • Enter clears owner search and restores full list — pressing Enter in a Dep Owners or Arr Owners search box clears the typed text and makes all owner rows visible again, preserving whatever was checked or unchecked during the filtered state.
v2.21.8 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Owner menus in region mode are now searchable — both the Dep Owners and Arr Owners dropdowns now include a text input at the top of the list. Typing letters instantly hides non-matching owner rows so you can find a specific pilot quickly in large regions. The search clears and the input is focused automatically each time the panel opens. The underlying checkbox filter, All/None buttons, and stripe-click behaviour are unaffected.
v2.21.7 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Weather dot vertical alignment on departure/arrival name lines — the flight-category dot next to the ICAO on the selected airport lines now sits 2 px higher, matching the alignment already applied to dots in the distance matrix.
v2.21.6 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix drag debounce set to 120 ms — reverted the adaptive debounce (which caused flickering on fast machines) in favour of a simple fixed 120 ms delay, down from the previous 160 ms.
v2.21.5 May 4, 2026 Improved
  • Adaptive matrix debounce during drag — the delay before the matrix recomputes while dragging a region ring was a fixed 160 ms. It now scales automatically between 40 ms and 160 ms based on how long the previous compute actually took (delay = last compute time × 3, clamped). Small matrices with few airports refresh near-instantly; large matrices self-regulate to avoid jank. Worst case remains 160 ms, so performance is always the same or better than before.
v2.21.4 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Region dot opacity matches background map dots — highlight dots inside region rings were slightly more opaque (0.92) than background dots (0.75 street / 0.88 satellite), causing a visible pop when rings moved over airports. Both layers now use the same fill opacity, so dots look identical whether inside a ring or not. Selected dots remain fully opaque.
v2.21.3 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Consistent dot z-ordering between map and region rings — airports in both the background dot layer and the region highlight layer are now rendered in ascending category order (cat 1 at the bottom, cat 7 on top). Previously the two layers used different iteration orders, so overlapping dots could swap stacking positions when a ring moved over them, causing a visible color flicker. Both layers now use the same sort, eliminating the artefact.
v2.21.2 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Selected region dot ring is now clearly visible — the white ring on a clicked dep/arr dot was hard to read. Selected dep dots now show a bright cyan ring (#00ffff), selected arr dots show a bright yellow ring (#ffff44), and both grow slightly to radius 12 to further distinguish the active selection. Unselected dots remain radius 9.
v2.21.1 May 4, 2026 Fix
  • Region airport dots same size as map dots — departure and arrival highlight dots inside region rings were previously rendered larger (radius 12, selected 14) than the standard background dots (radius 9). They now use the same radius 9, so the map has a uniform dot size throughout. Selected dots still show a bright white ring to indicate which row/column is active.
v2.21.0 May 4, 2026 Feature
  • Global map category filter in header bar — a new Map filter row (All / None / cat 1–7 / Invert) sits centred in the region modal header, giving you a map-wide category switch that hides background airport dots across the entire map regardless of region selection. Per-region Dep / Arr category filters continue to work independently and take precedence: if a region filter shows a category that the global filter has hidden, airports of that category still appear inside the region rings.
v2.20.1 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Distance slider rescales when Hide filtered airports is active — the slider's right (and left) edge now reflects only the distance range of airports still visible in the matrix. Previously the scale stayed anchored to the full dataset max even after hidden-category airports were removed by the Hide filtered toggle. Scale restores to the full range when Hide filtered is turned off.
  • File on SimBrief button text centred — the button label was left-aligned inside its full-width container; it is now horizontally centred.
v2.20.0 May 3, 2026 Feature
  • Minor version bump consolidating two weeks of region-mode and main-matrix improvements. Highlights below; granular per-version notes preserved in the entries that follow.
  • Region matrix sticky headers — column headers now stay pinned to the top during vertical scroll, row headers stay pinned to the left during horizontal scroll, and the corner cell stays anchored top-left in both directions. Multiple iterations led to a final root-cause fix: the column resize feature was setting inline position:relative on every <th> after render, silently overriding the CSS position:sticky rule. Sticky headers now work correctly in all configurations.
  • Pivot sort bullseye (⊙) — main distance matrix — the main matrix now has the same one-click pivot sort system as the region matrix. Click the ⊙ on any row or column header to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport: ⊙ → ▲ nearest → ▼ farthest → ⊙ clear. Replaces the older two-click sort pills + padlock system.
  • Main matrix typography & styling parity — ICAO labels now use Rajdhani (replacing Courier) and pick up category colors, matching the region matrix. Owner stripes, click-to-filter behavior, and free-space header click highlights all carry over from region mode.
  • Category filter split into Dep / Arr groups — the main matrix category filter now shows two independent sets of category buttons side-by-side, one for departures and one for arrivals, allowing cross-axis filtering. Buttons are sized down for side-by-side fit, "Inv" operates per group, and breathing space around the filter section was increased for clarity.
  • Light mode legibility overhaul — the dark-mode neon ICAO category palette (yellow, lime, cyan, etc.) was nearly unreadable on the cream light background. Light mode now uses a darker accessible palette — crimson, burnt orange, dark gold, forest green, dark teal, royal blue, dark magenta — across the region matrix, main distance matrix, and region-mode map dot tooltips. Dark mode keeps the original neon palette unchanged.
  • Owner stripe click-to-filter — click any owner stripe in the region matrix to filter to that owner only on that axis; click again to restore. Works for both departures and arrivals.
  • Region map dot selection ring — clicking an airport dot on the region map now draws a white ring around it, providing immediate visual confirmation of which airport is highlighted in the matrix.
  • Top menu buttons 50% larger; route strip 50% larger; owner profile links extended to cover the full Pilot Directory; ruler magnetic heading now uses the NOAA WMM API for sub-degree accuracy with cached results.
  • Numerous polish iterations — sort dot positioning, weather dot alignment, tooltip restrictions to ICAO/owner text, header redesigns across phases, dropdown composedPath fix, immediate stats updates, and many small spacing tweaks.
v2.19.55 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Main matrix dep sort dot nudge — departure row sort dots shifted 2px up and 1px right for tighter optical alignment with the ICAO label.
v2.19.54 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Light-mode ICAO category color legibility — replaced the dark-mode neon palette (cat 3 yellow, cat 5 cyan, cat 4 lime, etc.) with a darker accessible palette in light mode. The bright neon colors washed out against the cream background and made airport ICAO codes nearly impossible to read in the region matrix and main distance matrix. Each of the seven categories now uses a darker tone — crimson, burnt orange, dark gold, forest green, dark teal, royal blue, dark magenta — with strong contrast against the light background. Dark mode still uses the original neon palette unchanged.
  • Main matrix departure sort dot — nudged up another 2px (now sits 5px above vertical center) for better optical alignment with the ICAO label.
v2.19.53 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Region matrix sticky headers — root-cause fix — the column resize feature was setting th.style.position = 'relative' on every header cell after the matrix rendered. That inline style overrode the CSS position:sticky rule, silently turning sticky headers into ordinary relatively-positioned cells that scroll away with the rest of the table. Removed the override; position:sticky already creates a containing block for the absolutely-positioned resize handle, so the override was unnecessary as well as actively breaking the feature. Sticky now works in all three configurations: column headers stick to the top during vertical scroll, row headers stick to the left during horizontal scroll, and the corner cell stays pinned to the top-left during both.
v2.19.52 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Category filter breathing space +25% — increased the gap above the Dep/Arr category filter section (from the distance slider), the gap between the bubble groups and the Hide button, and the margin-bottom beneath the distance slider — providing clearer visual separation between all three UI groups.
v2.19.51 May 3, 2026 Fix
  • Dep sort dot nudged up 3px — main distance matrix departure row sort dots shifted up 3px from vertical center for better optical alignment with the ICAO label.
  • Weather dots nudged up 2px — wx condition dots in the main distance matrix headers shifted up 2px to sit more naturally alongside the ICAO text.
  • Category filter breathing space +20% — increased margin above the Dep/Arr category filter row and the gap between the filter groups and the action buttons below.
v2.19.50 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Split category filter into Dep / Arr groups — the distance matrix category filter now shows two independent sets of category buttons side-by-side, one for departure airports and one for arrivals. Filtering dep and arr independently lets you cross-filter by owner category on each axis without affecting the other.
  • No "Category" label — the group headers now read "Dep" and "Arr"; the word "Category" is omitted since the colored buttons make the purpose clear.
  • Smaller buttons, Inv shorthand — category buttons are slightly smaller to fit both groups side-by-side; the Invert button is shortened to "Inv" and each group's Inv operates on that group only.
v2.19.49 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Category colors in distance matrix headers — departure row labels and arrival column headers now use the same per-category color scheme as Region Mode (red through magenta for categories 1–7).
  • Rajdhani font for ICAO headers — departure and arrival ICAOs in the main distance matrix now use Rajdhani instead of Courier, matching the Region Mode matrix style.
  • Pivot sort bullseye (⊙) — each row and column header now shows a ⊙ sort dot. Click it to sort the opposite axis by distance to that airport — cycles nearest (▲), farthest (▼), then clears. Replaces the previous two-click sort pills and padlock system.
  • Dep sort dot nudge — Region Mode dep row sort indicator shifted one more pixel right.
v2.19.48 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Map dot selection ring — clicking a departure or arrival dot on the Region Mode map now shows a white ring around the selected dot, giving immediate visual confirmation of which airport is highlighted in the matrix.
  • Tooltip restricted to ICAO / owner text — the airport tooltip in the region matrix only appears when hovering the ICAO code or owner name text; moving the cursor to blank space in the same header cell hides it.
  • Sort dot nudge — dep row sort indicator shifted one pixel right.
v2.19.47 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Sticky headers — departure row and arrival column headers now stay pinned when scrolling the route matrix. The root cause was overflow:hidden on the modal acting as the sticky container; changing it to overflow:clip lets the inner scroll div take that role.
  • Route strip centered — the “{dep} → {arr} xxx nm” bar is now horizontally centered. Root cause: margin-left:auto on the Send button consumed all free space. Button is now position:absolute at the right edge.
v2.19.45 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Phase guidance text enlarged 50% — step badges and instruction text during region drawing are now 50% larger.
  • Owner profile tooltip gated — “Click to view SimFly profile” only appears in header tooltips when a URL is actually known for that owner.
  • ICAO click area tightened — ICAO link hit area is now text-width only; clicking blank space in a header cell fires the row/column highlight.
v2.19.44 May 3, 2026 Improved
  • Header tooltip distinguishes ICAO vs owner — hovering the ICAO shows “Click to view SimFly details”; hovering the owner name shows “Click to view SimFly profile”. Updates live as the cursor moves between them.
  • Free-space click highlights row / column — clicking blank space in a departure row or arrival column header highlights that airport’s row or column, matching the map dot behavior.
  • Main panel category buttons adopt per-category colors — the 1–7 buttons in the Distance Matrix panel now use the same color scheme as Region Mode (red, orange, yellow, green, cyan, blue, magenta).
v2.19.43 May 3, 2026 Feature
  • Owner stripe click-to-filter — clicking the colored owner ribbon on any column or row instantly filters the matrix to that owner. A second click restores the previous state. Active stripe shows a white inset outline.
  • Filter-then-cap — owner and category filters are applied before the per-region airport cap, so filtering frees cap slots for airports you want to see.
  • Region Mode header redesigned — Select Regions, Undo, and DONE buttons appear only in the phases where they are relevant.
  • Selected route strip enlarged 50% — ICAO codes, distance, and Send button are all 50% larger when a route is selected.
  • Airport dot tooltips improved — immediate on hover, darker background, cyan border glow.
  • Distance Map renamed to Route Map.
  • Owner dropdown stays open during filtering — fixed a bug where clicking a checkbox in the Dep/Arr Owners panel immediately closed it.
  • Route summary updates on filter change — dep × arr × routes count recalculates on every owner or category filter change, not only on region move.
  • Filtered airports removed from base map layer — filtered-out airports no longer appear as smaller dots alongside the active set.
v2.19.42 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner profile links cover full Pilot Directory — SimFly profile URLs from Recip Tracker’s Pilot Directory now appear on owner names throughout Active Airports, not just for directly tracked pilots.
v2.19.41 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Ruler magnetic heading uses NOAA WMM API — replaced the dipole approximation with a live NOAA World Magnetic Model call for sub-degree accuracy. Cached per origin; falls back to dipole with an ≈ prefix if unreachable.
v2.19.40 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Performance — owner data, canvas renderer, and tooltip HTML are all cached and reused across filter toggles and satellite switches, reducing GC pressure and redraw time.
v2.19.34–39 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Airport dot tooltips suppressed during region drawing — tooltips are correctly silent from Select Regions click through both region placements, then re-enabled once the matrix is visible. Required 6 iterations to fully neutralize Leaflet’s internal auto-open paths.
  • Max per region capped at 250 — removed the 500 and 1000 options to prevent out-of-memory crashes on large region overlap sets.
  • Owner filter preserves state on region resize — hidden owners stay hidden when the region is moved or resized; new owners entering the region appear visible by default.
v2.19.33 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter persists across region moves and resizes — when at least one owner is hidden in the Dep or Arr owner filter, dragging a region or resizing its radius now preserves your filter: previously-hidden owners stay hidden, and any new owners that enter the region appear visible by default. If all owners are currently visible (no filter active), the owner list auto-refreshes as before. Makes it easy to visually scan the map for a specific person’s airports by moving the region without losing your selection.
v2.19.32 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Route line always draws above the region hero line — the selected-route line and its distance label were sometimes hidden behind the center-to-center hero line, especially after dragging a region (which re-stacks the hero layers). Fixed by placing the route line in dedicated Leaflet panes at higher z-indices — routeLinePane (450, above the default overlay pane at 400) and routeLabelPane (650, above the marker pane at 600) — so the route is always on top regardless of draw order.
v2.19.31 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Sort menu reflects active distance pivot — clicking a dep or arr bullseye (⊙) to sort by distance now updates the Sort dropdown to read Closest ICAO or Furthest ICAO instead of still showing the previous mode. Choosing any option from the Sort dropdown while a pivot is active clears the pivot and returns to the selected global sort mode.
v2.19.30 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Same-owner dimming removed — cells where the departure and arrival airport share the same owner were previously dimmed and non-clickable on the basis that same-owner flights have no reciprocation value. That assumption was wrong, so the dim and the click block are gone. All cells are now equally selectable.
  • Pilot profile links in arrival column headers — owner name links in the arrival column headers were inconsistently absent due to a gist-load timing race. The matrix now always renders owner names as plain text first, then upgrades to profile links in a single post-render pass, so both departure rows and arrival columns get links under the same code path with no race condition.
v2.19.29 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Split owner filters — Dep Owners and Arr Owners — the single Owners dropdown has been replaced with two independent menus: Dep Owners (left of the Dep cat buttons) and Arr Owners (right of the Arr cat buttons). Selecting owners in each menu filters only the rows or columns for that side of the matrix, so you can mix and match — for example, showing departures from any owner but arrivals at airports owned by a specific pilot.
v2.19.28 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Escape from region selection at any time — the header Reset button has been renamed Cancel and now exits region selection mode entirely, returning the map to free exploration. Previously it only restarted the selection process (back to Step 1 crosshair) with no way to cancel short of drawing both regions and then using Close Results. Undo is unchanged — it still steps back one phase within the selection flow.
v2.19.27 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Cat filter groups inset toward center — the Dep and Arr category filter groups each have 100 px of inward margin, creating a visual buffer between the filter buttons and the centered route count.
v2.19.26 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Results bar reorganized with centered route count — the filtering bar above the matrix is now arranged as: Owners · Sort on the left, Dep category filters just left of center, the X dep × Y arr = Z routes summary in the true center (flex-grows to fill), Arr category filters just right of center, and Max per region · Close on the right. Max per region and Sort are separated to either side of the center stat.
v2.19.25 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix column headers now sticky — the arrival column header row was rendered without a <thead> element, which caused Chrome to ignore position:sticky; top:0 on those cells. Headers now render inside a proper <thead> so both the column headers (arr airports, vertical scroll) and row headers (dep airports, horizontal scroll) stick correctly as the matrix is scrolled.
  • Owner name hyperlinks restored in matrix headers — when the pilot-URL gist finished loading after the matrix had already rendered, the owner name spans were never upgraded to anchor links. Added a post-load refresh that walks the matrix and replaces any plain <span> owner labels with clickable links once the URL data becomes available.
v2.19.24 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter list scoped to current regions — the Owners dropdown now lists only pilots who own at least one airport in the departure or arrival region. Owners with no airports in either region are not shown. The list rebuilds automatically whenever the regions are recomputed (including handle drags and radius adjustments).
v2.19.23 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Map dot clicks accumulate dep + arr selection independently — clicking a departure airport dot highlights its matrix row without clearing any arrival column highlight, and vice versa. Each side remembers its last selection.
  • Intersection fires full cell selection — once both a departure and an arrival dot have been clicked, the matrix highlights the intersection cell, draws the route line, and shows the distance — identical to clicking that cell directly.
v2.19.22 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owners panel 50% larger — panel width, padding, font sizes, row height, color swatches, and All/None buttons all scaled up for easier readability and interaction.
  • Checked/unchecked state is now visually unambiguous — active owners show a filled cyan checkbox with a dark check mark; inactive owners show an empty box with a subtle border. No more relying on color alone to distinguish state.
v2.19.21 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft range ring is now fully non-interactive — the yellow range ring no longer captures mouse events, grabs focus, or shows a tooltip. It is display-only and no longer blocks map interaction.
  • Clicking a highlighted airport dot scrolls to its matrix row or column — clicking a dep airport dot highlights and scrolls to that departure row; clicking an arr airport dot highlights and scrolls to that arrival column.
  • Close Results button on results bar — a ✕ Close Results button on the right side of the results bar clears all selections and returns the map to free-explore mode, as if the modal had just opened.
v2.19.20 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Undo and Reset moved to upper-left header — both buttons are now in the region-controls group (left side), consistent with their role in the selection workflow rather than global controls.
  • Undo and Reset hidden until selection starts — buttons are not visible in the initial explore phase; they appear only after clicking Select Regions, so the header stays uncluttered until the region-drawing workflow begins.
v2.19.19 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Sort pivot (⊙) moved to top-right corner of header cells — was bottom-right, which caused overlap with owner name text. Increased font size by 50% (0.68 → 1.02 rem). Added right padding to all header cells to keep ICAO/owner text clear of the button.
  • Range ring tooltip no longer follows pointer — removed sticky:true so the tooltip only appears when hovering directly on the ring line; the permanent east-edge label is the primary distance indicator.
  • Header layout: region controls left, measurement controls right — Select Regions button and phase text are now left-justified; Ruler and ↩ Clear buttons are right-justified with a separator before Undo / Reset / Close.
  • Ruler label font 25% larger — measurement distance/bearing text increased from 0.75 to 0.94 rem for easier reading.
  • ↩ Clear removes one measurement at a time — each click undoes the most recently drawn measurement; repeated clicks walk back through the stack. The button disappears when all measurements have been removed.
v2.19.18 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Airport dots 3× larger — base dot radius increased from 3 px to 9 px, highlighted (in-region) dots from 4 px to 12 px, for easier visibility at typical zoom levels.
  • Ruler measurements persist until cleared — drag-releasing a measurement now freezes the line and circle in place. Each subsequent drag adds a new measurement overlay; all remain visible simultaneously. A ✕ Clear button appears in the header when any measurements exist and removes them all at once.
  • Ruler line contrast improved — changed color from washed-out light green to vivid purple (#cc44ff), which reads clearly on both light street maps and dark satellite imagery. Label background darkened with a matching border.
v2.19.17 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Free map exploration on open — Region Mode now opens in a neutral “explore” state so you can pan and zoom freely before committing to a selection. A new Select Regions button in the header initiates the departure/arrival drag flow when you’re ready.
  • Measurement ruler tool — a new Ruler toggle button in the header activates a live measurement overlay. Click anywhere on the map to anchor the origin; as you move the cursor, a radius spoke and range circle appear showing distance in nm, true bearing, and approximate magnetic bearing (simplified dipole model, accurate to ±5° for most regions). Click again to move the origin; click the button again to clear.
  • Per-column sort in the region matrix — every departure and arrival header cell now has a small ⊙ button in its corner. Clicking it sorts the opposite axis by distance to that airport: first click = nearest first (▲), second = farthest first (▼), third = clear (back to global sort). Active pivot is highlighted in the header.
v2.19.16 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Owners dropdown no longer hidden behind the map — the dropdown panel was being clipped by the modal’s overflow:hidden container. Changed to position:fixed with viewport-relative coordinates computed at open time, so the panel always renders above all Leaflet layers.
  • Range ring label no longer shows as a black box — the L.divIcon wrapper was producing a misaligned Leaflet container. Replaced with the same position:absolute;transform:translate() wrapper pattern used by the hero route badge, which renders cleanly with no background artifact.
  • Stale range ring glow halos removed on update — the soft glow halo circle under the range ring was not stored in a variable, so it was never removed when the ring updated (e.g., changing fuel or payload). The glow is now tracked in _rgRangeGlow and cleaned up on every ring update and on Undo/Reset.
v2.19.15 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Owner filter in Region Mode — a compact Owners ▾ dropdown in the results bar lets you show only selected owners’ airports in both the matrix and the map dots. Supports multi-select with checkboxes; All and None one-click accelerators; the button label changes to show the active count (e.g., “Owners: 3 / 7”) when filtering is active.
  • Matrix auto-expands with panel height — the distance matrix now fills all remaining space below the results bar rather than being capped at a fixed 38 vh, so dragging the split handle to grow the panel gives the matrix more visible rows.
  • Range ring visibility and label — the aircraft range ring is now thicker (3 px) with a soft gold glow halo underneath for better contrast. A permanent label at the ring’s east edge displays the range in nm, and hovering the ring shows a tooltip with the aircraft name.
v2.19.14 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Resizable map/matrix split — a thin drag handle now separates the Region Mode map from the results area. Drag it up or down to give more space to either the map or the matrix. The map redraws correctly after resize.
  • Global category colors on all airport dots — every airport dot on the Region Mode map now uses its SimFly category color (not just the highlighted region results). Airports with no category entry fall back to the previous blue/cyan tones. All dots carry the dark outline for satellite-imagery legibility.
  • Richer airport tooltips — dot tooltips now include the longest runway’s identifier pair, length in feet, surface type, and whether it is lighted, in addition to the existing ICAO, category, name, and owner fields.
  • Tooltip accessibility fix — the dark tooltip background was being overridden by Leaflet’s default white style. Fixed by strengthening the CSS selector specificity so the dark theme is applied consistently.
  • Consolidated filter row + All/None accelerators — the Dep and Arr category filter groups are now inline within the single results/controls bar instead of sitting in two separate rows below it. Each group gains All and None one-click buttons to select or clear all categories in that group at once.
v2.19.13 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft range ring in Region Mode — when an aircraft is selected in the main UI, placing the departure region center draws a dashed gold ring at that point showing the aircraft’s effective range in nm (matching the adjusted value shown in the aircraft panel — accounting for current fuel and payload settings). The ring updates live when the aircraft is changed or the fuel/payload sliders are moved, and clears on Undo or Reset.
v2.19.12 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region map dot tooltips — hovering any airport dot (both the background layer and the highlighted results layer) shows a tooltip after a 500 ms delay with the ICAO in its category color, airport name, and owner name. Tooltip cancels immediately on mouse-out or map pan, so accidental hovers never fire.
  • Category filter hides region dots from map — toggling a category button in the Dep or Arr filter bar now instantly removes matching airport dots from the map (in addition to hiding rows/columns in the matrix), keeping the map and table in sync.
v2.19.11 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region map dots: category colors — highlighted airport dots on the Region Mode map now use each airport’s SimFly category color (matching the matrix palette) rather than a flat cyan/orange. A dark outline is added on each dot so the colors stay legible over satellite imagery.
v2.19.10 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region Mode: separate Dep and Arr category filters — the category filter bar is now split into two independent rows: Dep filters departure rows (and their map dots) and Arr filters arrival columns (and their map dots). Each has its own Invert button. Filtering one side no longer affects the other.
  • Region map dots respond to category filter — toggling a category button now hides or shows the corresponding airport dots on the Leaflet map in real time, keeping the map in sync with the matrix.
  • DEP/ARR corner cell now resizable — dragging the right edge of the corner cell resizes the entire departure-name column (corner + all row headers together). Double-click to auto-fit all columns and rows as before.
v2.19.9 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Region matrix: KML Generator category colors — ICAO headers in the region matrix now use the same seven-color palette as KML Generator's “Both” mode (red through magenta for categories 1–7), replacing the aircraft-tab color set.
  • Max per region: 5 and 10 options added — the dropdown now includes 5 and 10 at the top for small, focused region comparisons.
  • Sort: Group by category — a new “Group by category” sort option clusters departure rows by their airport category (1 through 7), then alphabetically within each group.
  • Category filter bar in Region Mode — the same category filter bubbles from the main UI appear below the results bar. Toggle individual category buttons to show/hide departure and arrival airports by category, or hit Invert to flip the selection. Resets to all-on when the region is cleared.
  • Route info label on map — selecting a matrix cell now shows a floating label at the route midpoint displaying departure ICAO, arrival ICAO, and distance in nm.
v2.19.8 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Monospaced distance numbers — all distance values in the region matrix cells, the drag radius label, the hero line midpoint badge, and the selected-route nm display now use Courier New for clean column alignment and unambiguous digit rendering.
v2.19.7 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region circles: drag to move and resize — once both regions are set, grab the center pin of either circle and drag to reposition it, or grab the cyan/orange handle at the circle’s east edge to resize the radius. The matrix refreshes in real time as you drag (debounced) and does a final full refresh on release. Hit areas are generously sized (20 px for center, 16 px for edge handle) — no pixel-perfect targeting required.
  • Region matrix: resizable columns and rows — every column header now has a drag handle on its right edge, and every row header has one on its bottom edge. Drag to resize. Double-click any handle to auto-fit all columns and rows to their natural content width, which also removes the 108 px truncation cap on owner names.
v2.19.6 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Same-owner cells: tooltip explanation — dimmed same-owner matrix cells now show a native tooltip ("Same owner — no reciprocation value") on hover so the visual treatment is self-documenting.
  • Same-owner cells receive row/column highlight — previously, selecting a cell excluded same-owner cells from the column and row highlight. They now receive the same cyan tint as other cells in the selected row/column — only the diagonal “same airport” cells remain un-highlighted.
  • Info bar enlarged 50% — the results summary, Max per region, and Sort controls are now rendered at 1.5× their previous font size for easier reading at a glance.
  • Region matrix ICAO and owner headers are clickable — each ICAO label in the row and column headers now links to its SimFly details page (simfly.io/assets/airport/…/details) in a new tab. Owner name labels link to the owner’s SimFly profile when a profile URL is on record; owners without a profile URL remain plain text.
v2.19.5 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region state persists across modal close/reopen — closing and reopening the Region modal no longer resets the map. Both region circles, the hero line, the dot highlights, and the matrix all survive and are immediately visible on reopen. Only the Reset button clears the state.
  • Airport dot visibility on satellite — base airport dots were near-invisible on Esri satellite imagery. On satellite mode, dots now render in light blue (#a8d8f0) with a white stroke; dots are automatically re-rendered when switching tile layers.
  • Cell selection highlights route on map — clicking a matrix cell now draws a gold route line (three-layer: amber glow, mid glow, white core) between the two specific departure and arrival airports on the map, with cyan/orange endpoint rings. Clears when the matrix rebuilds or Reset is pressed.
  • Matrix sort controls — new Sort selector in the results bar. Default is A–Z alphabetical by ICAO; Group by owner clusters same-owner airports together, then sorts alphabetically within each owner. Applies to both rows and columns independently.
v2.19.4 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region matrix: ICAO category colors fixed — categoryDB is a let binding and not on window, so window.categoryDB was always undefined; corrected to direct reference so per-category colors now render correctly on all header ICAO labels.
  • Region matrix: row and column highlights on cell click — clicking a cell now highlights the full departure row and arrival column (matching the main matrix behavior) in addition to the selected-cell highlight. Highlights clear when a new cell is clicked.
  • Send to Flight Planner: auto-selects owners and highlights route — after populating the departure and arrival ICAO fields, the button now fires onDistInput for both fields, which auto-selects the correct owner in each dropdown, renders the main matrix, and highlights the departure row and arrival column — exactly as if the user had typed the ICAOs manually.
  • Region map: tile layer toggle — a 🛰 Satellite / 🗺 Street button now appears in the top-right corner of the region map, matching the Distance Map toggle. Default remains street/chart view.
  • Region hero line: badge centering fixed — the nm distance badge at the midpoint was offset because transform:translate(-50%,-50%) was on a display:block span inside the Leaflet DivIcon; moved the transform to a wrapper div with iconSize:[0,0] so the badge now centers precisely on the midpoint.
v2.19.3 May 2, 2026 Fix
  • Region matrix: enlarged text and cells — all matrix font sizes, padding, and header widths increased by 50% for legibility. ICAO headers: 1.2rem; owner sub-labels: 0.93rem; data cells: 1.2rem base with 0.3rem × 0.68rem padding; row header min-width: 108px.
v2.19.2 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region Mode: hero route line — once both regions are confirmed, a glowing center-to-center line connects the two region centers with a three-layer stack (wide cyan bloom, mid glow, bright white core) matching the Distance Map hero line style. A floating badge at the midpoint shows the exact nm distance between centers. Line and badge are removed when the arrival region is undone or reset.
v2.19.1 May 2, 2026 Improved
  • Region matrix: owner color bands — each column and row header now shows a thick colored accent border derived from a consistent per-owner hash. Owners are visually distinct at a glance without needing to read the name.
  • Region matrix: two-line headers — column and row headers now show the ICAO code (colored by SimFly aircraft category) on the first line and the owner name in small muted text below, matching the visual language of the main matrix.
  • Region matrix: same-owner cell dimming — cells where departure and arrival are owned by the same pilot are dimmed and non-clickable, so cross-owner reciprocation opportunities stand out immediately.
  • Region matrix: header tooltips — hovering any ICAO header cell shows the full SimFly airport tooltip (name, category, elevation, weather, owner) via the existing tooltip engine.
v2.19.0 May 2, 2026 Feature
  • Region Mode — map-based airport selection — new ⊙ Regions button in the panel header opens a full-screen interactive map modal. Click & drag to draw a departure region circle (radius shown live in nm with a radius line), then use the measuring ruler to place and size an arrival region. Both regions are drawn from the SimFly owned-airport database. Once both are set, a filterable distance matrix renders showing all owned airports within each region with nm distances between them. Cells are sticky-header scrollable.
  • Cell selection and Send to Flight Planner — clicking any matrix cell highlights it and shows a route card with the departure airport, arrival airport, and distance. The Send to Flight Planner → button populates the main ICAO fields and closes the modal.
  • Configurable airport cap — results default to the 100 closest owned airports per region; adjustable to 25, 50, 100, 200, 500, or 1000 via the cap selector. A performance warning appears above 200.
  • Highlight layer — after both regions are confirmed, owned airports within each region are highlighted cyan (departure) and orange (arrival) on the map to confirm the selection visually.
  • Undo / Reset controls — Undo walks back one phase (ruler → dep region, results → ruler); Reset clears everything and returns to the drawing start state. Existing owner-based matrix and all other functionality are unchanged.
v2.18.1 Apr 30, 2026 Improved
  • Light mode: cream background — replaced the blue-tinted white palette (#eef2f7 bg, #ffffff surfaces) with warm cream (#f5f2ea bg, #faf8f3 surfaces). Distance matrix row/column highlights and hover states updated to matching warm tones.
  • Light mode: font weight improvements — Orbitron elements that defaulted to weight 400 (tabs, buttons, selects, theme toggle) bumped to 600; Rajdhani body text bumped to 500 for better legibility on Windows.
v2.18.0 Apr 29, 2026 Feature
  • Weather summary in Distance Map info cards — when a route is highlighted on the distance map, the Departure and Arrival info cards beneath the map now include a Weather section with the same content as the badge-hover tooltip (raw METAR, flight category, wind, visibility, sky, temp/dewpoint, altimeter, observed time). The section is omitted entirely when no METAR is cached for that airport, so cards stay compact for unfetched airports.
  • Owner names link to SimFly profile pages (easter-egg) — when an owner has a SimFly profile URL recorded in the Recip Tracker Gist (the SimFly Profile URL field on the Edit Pilot modal), Active Airports now uses that URL in two places: the Owner: Name line on the matrix tooltips and Distance Map info cards, and the large Departure/Arrival Airport Owner dropdown labels. The links are styled to be visually identical to plain text — no underline, inherited color — so they don't clutter the UI; pro users discover them via the pointer cursor on hover. Owners without a recorded URL render as plain static text.
  • Pilot URL data shared with Active Airports — Active Airports already loaded the Recip Tracker Gist for the 3-letter pilot codes used in SimBrief flight numbers; that fetch now also captures each pilot's SimFly profile URL into window._gistPilotUrls. Failures stay silent; missing URLs fall back to plain-text rendering.
v2.17.0 Apr 29, 2026 Feature
  • Aircraft suitability now corrects for density altitude — required takeoff and landing runway distances were previously sea-level book numbers, which underestimated requirements at hot/high airports. A flight from SEQM (≈9,228 ft elevation) revealed the gap. Suitability dimming and the matrix tooltip's runway verdict lines now apply a per-SimFly-category DA factor on top of the existing weight scaling: light pistons (Cat 1) take the biggest hit at ~12% per 1,000 ft DA on takeoff, jets (Cat 5–7) ~7–8%, and landing distance scales at ~3.5–4% across all categories.
  • METAR-driven DA when weather is fetched, ISA fallback otherwise — when the ⛅ button has fetched a station's METAR, the airport's reported temperature and altimeter setting (inHg or hPa, with fall-through conversion) drive a true DA calculation including pressure altitude. When no METAR is cached, suitability falls back to ISA conditions computed from field elevation alone. Stale METARs (past the 30-minute fresh window) are still used — far better than ISA. DA is floored at zero so cold or sea-level airports never reduce required distance below book numbers.
  • Suitability tooltip shows DA value and source — the runway verdict line now ends with a DA annotation: DA 11,400 ft (METAR, 14:30Z) for direct METARs (using the published observation time in Zulu, matching the format on the existing weather tooltip), DA 11,400 ft (METAR via SEQU, 18 nm, 14:00Z) for nearest-station fallbacks, or DA 9,228 ft (ISA — fetch weather for accuracy) when no weather is loaded. The ISA form is a quiet nudge, not a warning. If a METAR is missing its observation timestamp, the annotation falls back to elapsed time since fetch.
  • Suitability tooltips refresh when weather is fetched — clicking ⛅ now triggers a matrix rebuild after the METAR fetch completes, so cached row and column tooltips pick up the freshly-computed DA values immediately. Suitability dimming also re-evaluates because a new METAR can flip an airport from suitable to unsuitable (e.g. when temps push DA above what the longest runway can support at the current weight).
  • At-MTOW reference in the suitability tooltip is now DA-corrected — the parenthetical book number that anchored the displayed required value used to be sea-level, so a reader at less than 100% TOW saw an apples-to-oranges comparison (DA-corrected required vs. sea-level reference). Both numbers now share a basis at this airport's DA, and the parenthetical reads (X ft at MTOW · DA-corrected) to make the basis explicit.
  • Spec strip Adj load disclaimer extended — the planning disclaimer below the Aircraft panel now notes that Adj load figures are weight-corrected at sea level only; per-airport DA correction lives in the matrix tooltips and dimming. Avoids confusion when the Adj load number differs from the matrix tooltip's required value at the same airport.
  • Distance slider range marker label simplified — the aircraft code label above the slider now reads C750 Range instead of C750 Max Range; the marker arrow already conveys it's the maximum.
  • Matrix corner cell label refreshed — the top-left cell now reads DEP ↓ / ARR → on two lines with directional arrows, replacing the plain Dep / Arr text. Conveys row/column orientation at a glance.
  • Distance Map gets its own ⇄ Swap button — a centered swap button now sits beneath the map panel, mirroring the owner-swap button between the dropdowns: same _distSwapOwners() handler and same title behavior (different-owner case shows "Swap Departure and Arrival owners"; same-owner case shows "Same owner selected — swap has no effect"). Saves a long scroll back to the matrix when working in the map view.
v2.16.14 Apr 26, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO swap button now correctly updates the Distance Map — v2.16.13 added a _dmRenderMap() call to _distSwapIcaos, but the map still showed the pre-swap route. Root cause: the map determines the active departure and arrival from matrix row/column highlights (or pins), not from the ICAO text fields directly. Since ICAO swap only updates the text fields, the stale highlights drove the map render. Fixed by reading the ICAO text field values in _dmRenderMap as a new step in the priority chain — between "sole airport" and "matrix highlight" — gated on the ICAO being present in the owner's airport set. Text field and highlight are always in sync in every other path, so no existing behaviour is affected.
v2.16.13 Apr 26, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO swap button now updates the Distance Map — clicking ⇄ between the ICAO fields swapped the departure and arrival values and recalculated distance, but never re-rendered the map. The hero route line and active-departure marker therefore continued showing the pre-swap airports. Fixed by calling _dmRenderMap() at the end of _distSwapIcaos, matching the behaviour of the owner swap which re-renders via renderDistMatrix.
v2.16.12 Apr 25, 2026 Fix
  • Owner swap button no longer dims when same owner is selected — the button was styled to appear disabled (low opacity, default cursor) when both dropdowns had the same owner, but the button remained fully clickable, creating a misleading affordance. Removed the same-owner CSS class and its opacity/cursor overrides; the button now stays fully lit at all times. The tooltip still changes to "Same owner selected — swap has no effect" as a lightweight hint.
v2.16.11 Apr 25, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO swap button now enables correctly after map or matrix selection — clicking a departure or arrival map marker, clicking a distance line, or clicking a cell in the distance matrix all set the ICAO fields directly (bypassing the normal oninput handler to avoid a matrix re-render). These paths did not call the swap-button state refresh, so the ⇄ button stayed disabled even with both fields populated. Fixed by calling _updateLookupBtns() in both _distClickCell and _distSyncInputsFromSelection.
v2.16.10 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Removed commented-out KML Load Panel — the full manual file-upload UI block (54 lines) left over from the KML Generator origin was deleted from the HTML. All surrounding live code (the hidden #importResultPreview data source, JavaScript functions with null guards, and CSS rules) is unchanged and fully functional.
v2.16.9 Apr 24, 2026 Improved
  • Help modal updated — added Weather & Flight Conditions and Distance Map sections to document features added since the help was last written; corrected the ⇄ Swap button description to reflect its current location between the owner dropdowns rather than the matrix corner.
v2.16.8 Apr 24, 2026 Improved
  • Weather dots on dep/arr name lines now show METAR tooltip on hover — hovering the flight-category dot in the departure or arrival name line now shows the same decoded weather tooltip (conditions, wind, visibility, ceiling, raw METAR) as the matrix dots. Moving off the dot back onto the airport name restores the SimFly airport details tooltip; the SimFly link itself is unchanged.
v2.16.7 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Weather dot spacing balanced — removed the extra space before the dot (left gap) and pulled the em dash 2px closer (right gap) so the dot sits evenly between the ICAO text and the separator.
v2.16.6 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Weather dot spacing fixed for arrival line — the same dot-overwriting-dash regression from v2.16.5 affected the arrival name line; the departure fix missed the arrival template variable. Both lines now read cleanly as ICAO ● — Airport Name.
v2.16.5 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Weather dot alignment corrected — the flight-category dot was appearing flush against the em dash with no gap, causing it to visually overwrite the separator. Added spacing between the dot and the dash so it reads cleanly as ICAO ● — Airport Name.
v2.16.4 Apr 24, 2026 Improved
  • Weather dots appear on Departure and Arrival name lines — after clicking Get Weather with ICAOs typed in the text fields, a flight-category dot (VFR/MVFR/IFR/LIFR) now appears inline between the ICAO and airport name for both departure and arrival, even when no owner matrix is displayed. A loading spinner appears during the fetch, and cached data populates immediately when ICAOs are re-entered.
v2.16.3 Apr 24, 2026 Improved
  • Get Weather button disabled until an ICAO is available — the ⛅ button is now greyed out on load and stays disabled until at least one valid ICAO appears in the Departure or Arrival field, or the matrix is populated. It re-enables immediately as you type and disables again if both fields are cleared.
v2.16.2 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Weather button now anchors to panel top — removed align-self:center from the .btn-wx-hdr CSS rule, which was overriding the grid's align-items:start and keeping the button mid-height regardless of the grid setting.
v2.16.1 Apr 24, 2026 Fix
  • Weather button and action buttons anchored to panel top — changed grid from align-items:end to align-items:start so the ⛅ button, Help, Reset, and File on SimBrief all align to the top of the header row at all panel widths, instead of drifting downward when the right column is taller than the ICAO fields.
v2.16.0 Apr 24, 2026 Improved
  • Get Weather button moved to panel center — the ⛅ button now sits in the middle column of the sticky header between the ICAO fields and the action buttons, always visible regardless of whether an owner matrix is showing.
  • Weather fetch works without matrix — clicking ⛅ now fetches weather for any ICAOs typed directly into the Departure and Arrival fields, even when no owner dropdown is selected and no matrix is rendered.
  • Corner cell replaced with Dep / Arr label — the top-left matrix cell (vacated by the weather button) now shows a subtle "Dep / Arr" orientation label.
  • Top panel height reduced 10% — vertical padding trimmed (1rem→0.9rem top, 0.65rem→0.6rem bottom) to reveal more of the app below the header without crowding any elements.
  • ICAO swap button disabled until an ICAO is entered — the ⇄ button between the Departure and Arrival ICAO fields is now disabled (greyed out) on load and re-enables as soon as either field contains a value.
v2.15.12 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • Lookup buttons restyled to match filter button family — the 🔍 buttons beside Departure and Arrival labels are now 25% smaller (0.75rem) and use the same cyan-tint background, low-opacity border, and opacity-based hover/disabled states as the Get Weather corner button and category filter buttons.
v2.15.11 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO swap button aligned with inputs — restructured the departure/arrival fields to a CSS grid (two rows: labels, then inputs + swap button), so the ⇄ button sits inline with the textboxes rather than floating at mid-height of the label+input stack.
v2.15.10 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • Map fits all airports on marker click — clicking any departure or arrival marker now zooms and pans to fit the full set of visible dep and arr airports in view, rather than centering on the single clicked airport.
  • ICAO field layout reverted — the flex:1 growth applied to dep/arr fields in v2.15.9 pushed the swap button far right; reverted to natural field widths.
v2.15.9 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • ICAO swap button centered between inputs — both Departure and Arrival field containers now grow equally (flex:1) so the ⇄ button lands exactly between the two textboxes regardless of label width differences.
  • Owner swap button dims when both dropdowns are empty — the ⇄ button between the owner dropdowns now correctly fades out on page load and any time both fields are blank, not just when the same owner is explicitly selected in both.
v2.15.8 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Arrival map marker restored to light grey — arrival dots were visually indistinguishable from the new dark-grey departure fill; changed to #c0c0c0 (light silver) so the dep/arr contrast is clear again.
  • ICAO swap button vertical position corrected — the ⇄ button between the ICAO fields now aligns to the bottom of the field stack (inline with the textboxes) rather than floating at mid-height.
v2.15.7 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • ICAO swap button vertically centered — the ⇄ button between the Departure and Arrival ICAO fields now sits mid-height of the label+input stack rather than aligning to the bottom edge.
  • ICAO field gap reduced — horizontal spacing between the Departure and Arrival ICAO inputs reduced by 40% for a tighter layout.
  • Departure map marker changed to dark grey — departure dots on the weather map now use dark grey (#3a3a3a) instead of black, reducing harsh contrast and easing readability for users sensitive to high-contrast dot patterns.
v2.15.6 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • Lookup buttons moved to label row — the 🔍 magnifying-glass buttons for Departure and Arrival now sit inline to the right of their respective labels rather than beside the ICAO textboxes, keeping the input row clean.
v2.15.5 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • Weather corner button shows cloud icon only — text removed; state (fresh/stale) is conveyed by border color; hovering shows a tooltip with the fetch time and a click-to-refresh hint.
  • Duplicate Get Weather button removed — the standalone button that appeared above the map panel has been eliminated; the corner cell is now the sole entry point.
  • ICAO swap button replaces the → arrow — the right-pointing arrow between the Departure and Arrival ICAO inputs is replaced by the same ⇄ pill used between the owner dropdowns. Clicking it swaps both ICAO values (and any associated pins) and immediately recalculates distance, making it possible to reverse a manually entered route without a matrix being open.
v2.15.4 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Weather corner button no longer clips text — button state is now preserved across matrix re-renders, text wraps correctly at all states, and fresh/stale states show a compact two-line format (e.g. "✓ Wx / 22:04Z ↻") that fits the fixed-width column.
  • Corner button restyled to match category filter aesthetic — the button uses muted tones (transparent background, low-opacity border, muted text) rather than the prominent teal color, keeping the corner visually calm while still showing idle / fetching / fresh / stale states.
v2.15.3 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Weather button contained within corner cell — the button is now wrapped in a fixed-width inner div so it cannot bleed over adjacent column headers regardless of text length.
  • Same-owner badge removed; Swap button dims instead — the "SAME OWNER" label no longer appears in the corner cell. When both owner dropdowns are set to the same value the ⇄ Swap button fades out and its tooltip changes to "Same owner — swap has no effect".
v2.15.2 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Weather corner button no longer widens matrix column — the corner cell is now capped at 72 px wide and the button wraps its text within that constraint, keeping the matrix layout stable when weather data is loaded.
v2.15.1 Apr 23, 2026 Improved
  • Map weather refresh fixed — clicking "Get Weather" while the Distance Map was already open did not update marker colors. The post-fetch re-render now correctly detects map open state and triggers a redraw.
  • Map tooltip role now correct — all map marker tooltips previously showed "Departure" regardless of role. Arrival-only airports now show "Arrival"; airports that appear in both dep and arr lists (same-owner mode) show "Departure / Arrival".
  • Swap button moved between owner dropdowns — the ⇄ Swap control has been relocated from the top-left corner of the matrix to sit inline between the Departure and Arrival owner selectors, making its function more obvious.
  • Get Weather button moved to matrix corner — the weather button now occupies the top-left corner cell of the distance matrix, freeing the controls row and keeping the button visible whenever the matrix is shown. Button state (idle / fetching / fresh / stale) is preserved across matrix re-renders.
  • Map marker colors revised — departure dots now use black fill; arrival dots use medium grey. Both remain clearly distinct from the four weather-category ring colors (VFR green, MVFR blue, IFR red, LIFR magenta).
v2.15.0 Apr 23, 2026 Feature
  • Live weather overlay via CheckWX API — a new "Get Weather" button appears below the category filters whenever a matrix is displayed. Clicking it fetches current METARs for all visible airports in batches of 25, firing all batches simultaneously so a full grid resolves in a single round-trip. Results are cached for 30 minutes; the button shows a live timestamp and transitions from green (fresh) to amber (stale) as data ages.
  • Flight category badges in matrix headers — each row and column header gains a small filled dot once weather is loaded: green (VFR), blue (MVFR), red (IFR), magenta (LIFR). Airports with no direct METAR automatically fall back to the nearest reporting station within 100 nm; fallback badges render as an outlined ring to distinguish them from direct observations.
  • Rich weather tooltip on badge hover — hovering any weather dot shows the raw METAR string followed by a decoded breakdown: flight category (color-coded), wind, visibility, sky conditions with cloud heights, temperature/dewpoint, altimeter setting, and observation time in Zulu. Nearest-station fallbacks include the station ICAO and distance.
  • Weather-aware map markers — Distance Map dots use a dual-encoding scheme: fill color identifies role (orange = departure, silver = arrival) and the border ring indicates weather category (green = VFR, blue = MVFR, red = IFR, magenta = LIFR). Dots are 50% larger than before and borders are 25% thicker for easier reading. Nearest-station fallbacks use a dashed ring. Hovering a marker shows a rich tooltip with airport name, role, full weather breakdown, and the raw METAR at the bottom. A two-section legend in the map corner explains both the fill and ring encodings.
  • Cache invalidates on owner change — switching either the departure or arrival owner dropdown clears cached weather and resets the button to "Get Weather", ensuring stale data from a previous fleet is never displayed against a new airport set.
v2.14.6 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Runway condition codes expanded in tooltip — supplement records store condition as a separate single-letter field (E/G/F/P). The tooltip was rendering it raw, e.g. "Turf (G)", instead of expanding it through the same condition map used for OurAirports compound strings. Now renders "Turf (Good condition)" consistent with the OurAirports path.
v2.14.5 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Runway supplement data now supersedes OurAirports in tooltip, suitability, and dimming — all three runway lookup chains checked OurAirports first and only fell back to manually supplemented data when OurAirports had nothing. Airports with corrections or enrichments saved via the KML Generator Edit Airport tab (e.g. corrected surface type, added width) were therefore invisible — the tooltip always showed the original OurAirports values. Priority is now reversed: supplement data is checked first, OurAirports used only when no supplement exists.
v2.14.4 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Footer owner count corrected — the owner count in the app footer now excludes comment lines, section headers, and entries with no airports. Previously the footer counted every non-empty line in the data file, inflating the number by including non-owner entries.
v2.14.3 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Distance Map panel header matches Aircraft panel styling — removed the map emoji from the header label, changed casing to all-caps "DISTANCE MAP", and aligned hover behaviour to the subtle background tint used by the Aircraft panel. Previously the Distance Map header changed border and text color on hover; it now behaves identically to Aircraft in both dark and light mode.
v2.14.2 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Distance Map panel header font matches Aircraft panel — the "Distance Map" toggle header now uses the Orbitron typeface with matching letter-spacing, consistent with the Aircraft panel header and other section labels throughout the app.
v2.14.1 Apr 23, 2026 Fix
  • Map correctly hides out-of-range arrivals when a departure is selected — when both "Filter airports unsuitable for aircraft" and "Hide filtered airports" are active, clicking a departure marker on the Distance Map now removes arrival markers that the selected aircraft cannot reach from that specific departure. Previously, an arrival stayed visible on the map as long as any other departure could reach it, regardless of whether the active departure was within range. Two issues fixed: (1) the map was reading stale filter state before the new departure context was applied; (2) column-level hiding alone was insufficient — the fix adds a per-route cell check so each arrival is evaluated against the actual active departure.
v2.14.0 Apr 23, 2026 Feature
  • Automatic update notifications — on every load the app silently checks the GitHub releases page for a newer version. If one is available, a small orange badge appears next to the version label in the header showing the new version number. Clicking the badge opens the GitHub releases page to download it. The badge can be dismissed with the × button and will not reappear for that version; a subsequent release will trigger it again.
v2.13.10 Apr 22, 2026 Fix
  • Distance readout wraps below ICAO inputs at narrow widths — the departure and arrival fields, arrow, and nm figure are now grouped into an inner flex container that wraps internally. At narrow screen widths the nm figure drops to a second line within that group rather than displacing the Help, Reset, and File on SimBrief buttons.
v2.13.9 Apr 22, 2026 Improved
  • Flight Planner header height reduced ~30% — removed the "Flight Planner" title row; Help and Reset buttons are now tucked into a compact row directly above the File on SimBrief button. Top and bottom padding on the sticky panel also reduced, reclaiming meaningful vertical screen space without crowding any controls.
v2.13.8 Apr 22, 2026 Feature
  • Airport info panel below the Distance Map — when both a departure and arrival airport are selected (hero route visible), a side-by-side panel appears beneath the map showing full airport details for each. Departure is on the left, arrival on the right. Each card shows the same information as the matrix header tooltips: airport name, ICAO/IATA, city, owner, SimFly category, elevation, runway data, and aircraft suitability verdict. The panel hides automatically when no hero route is active.
v2.13.7 Apr 21, 2026 Fix
  • Clock widget readable in light theme — the LCL/UTC overlay in the top-left corner now uses dark navy text and a subtle blue-tinted background when light mode is selected. Previously the pale-green text on a semi-transparent dark panel was hard to read against the light page background.
  • Release notes filter buttons visible in light theme — inactive and active filter buttons in the release notes panel now render with blue-tinted fills and borders instead of the near-invisible white-on-white rgba values used by the dark-mode defaults.
v2.13.6 Apr 21, 2026 Improved
  • Clicking a distance line selects the route as the hero — clicking any line on the map now highlights the corresponding departure row and arrival column in the matrix, making it the hero route (cyan glow). Previously clicking a line only showed a tooltip and left an ugly focus box around the route.
  • Focus outline suppressed on map lines — removed Leaflet's default SVG focus rectangle that appeared around clicked polylines.
v2.13.5 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • Distance Map honors "Hide filtered airports" setting — when the Hide filtered airports toggle is active, airports that have been hidden from the matrix are also removed from the map. Markers for hidden departure or arrival airports are not plotted, and any distance lines to/from those airports are omitted. The map viewport refits automatically when the visible airport set changes.
v2.13.4 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • Distance lines thicker and easier to hover — visual line weight increased from 1.5 to 3; each line is backed by an invisible 18px-wide buffer polyline that acts as the hover/tooltip target, making lines easy to select even at small zoom levels.
  • Hero route highlight — when both a departure row and an arrival column are selected in the matrix, the line connecting those two airports is rendered as a bright solid cyan line with a wide glow halo instead of a dashed white line, drawing attention to the active route. Status bar shows the highlighted ICAO pair. Selecting just a departure (no arrival column) shows a hint to click an arrival column.
v2.13.3 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • Distance lines restored; sticky-header z-index fix corrected — v2.13.2 placed the stacking context on .dist-map-body, which also has overflow: hidden; that combination made the element a containing block, causing it to clip Leaflet's SVG overlay pane (which intentionally extends slightly outside the map container for smooth panning). Fixed by moving position: relative; z-index: 1 to .dist-map-panel, which has no overflow clipping, so the stacking context contains Leaflet's internal z-indices without intercepting its SVG geometry.
v2.13.2 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • Map no longer occludes sticky Flight Planner header when scrolling — Leaflet's internal elements (marker pane, zoom control) carry z-indices up to 1000, which caused them to render above the sticky header (z-index: 20) when the page was scrolled. Fixed by adding position: relative; z-index: 1 to the map body container, establishing a CSS stacking context that contains all of Leaflet's internal z-indices below the sticky panel.
v2.13.1 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • Map markers are clickable — clicking a departure airport marker highlights its row in the distance matrix and redraws distance lines from that airport; clicking an arrival airport marker highlights its column. Both actions scroll the matrix to the selection and pan the map to the clicked airport.
v2.13.0 Apr 20, 2026 Feature
  • Distance Map panel — a collapsible map panel now appears below the distance matrix whenever departure and arrival owners are selected. Powered by Leaflet 1.9.4 via CDN (no API key required) with Esri satellite and CARTO street tile layers. Departure airports are plotted as blue dot markers; arrival airports as green dots. Dashed distance lines are drawn from the active departure airport (pinned, sole, or row-highlighted) to all arrival airports; hovering a line shows the ICAO pair and nautical-mile distance. The panel is collapsed by default — the map is not loaded or rendered until expanded, saving processing. Viewport auto-fits to the plotted airports on the first render for a given owner pair; subsequent row-highlight clicks redraw the lines without resetting the user's pan/zoom. A tile-layer toggle button switches between satellite and street views.
v2.12.9 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • Paved surface suitability check for generic codes — airports with OurAirports surface codes PAVED, TAR, or PER were displaying a paved runway label but still triggering the "No paved runway" suitability warning. These codes are now recognised as paved in the aircraft suitability check, consistent with how they display in the runway tooltip.
v2.12.8 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • Sticky Flight Planner header — the panel title, departure/arrival inputs, distance readout, File on SimBrief button, and airport name lines are now sticky at the top of the viewport while the aircraft selector, owner dropdowns, and distance matrix scroll beneath them. A border separates the fixed header from the scrollable content below.
v2.12.7 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • No confirmation dialog for users without a PAT — the new-pilot-code dialog (v2.12.6) is only shown when a GitHub PAT is available in storage. Without a PAT the app silently generates temporary codes and files on SimBrief directly, keeping the experience frictionless for shared/distributed use.
v2.12.6 Apr 20, 2026 Feature
  • Auto-generate pilot codes for unregistered owners — when either airport owner has no 3-letter code in the Recip Tracker Gist, a code is generated on the fly using the same algorithm as the Recip Tracker (first-3-letters priority, then positional combos, then word-initial combos, then brute-force unique permutations). Codes for a dep/arr pair are generated conflict-free even when both are new.
  • Confirmation dialog before filing — if either owner is missing a registered code, a modal lists the generated codes and asks whether to persist them. Requires a PAT in localStorage to offer the save option; without one the dialog still shows but only offers filing without saving.
  • New pilots added to Recip Tracker Gist on confirm — when confirmed, the app reads the current Recip Gist, appends the new pilot entries (username, code, zero totals), and PATCHes the file. The local _gistPilotCodes map is updated immediately so the same session doesn't re-prompt for the same pilot. Gist write failures are silent — SimBrief is always opened regardless.
v2.12.5 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief fltnum now clears on route change — when a flight number was pre-supplied via URL parameters (launched from Recip Tracker), it was never cleared when the user subsequently changed dep/arr by typing in the fields, clicking a matrix cell, or clicking a row/column label. The URL-provided fltnum would persist for a completely different route. _urlFltNum is now cleared in all three user-initiated route-change paths (onDistInput, _distClickCell, _distSyncInputsFromSelection), guarded so URL param application at load time is not affected.
v2.12.4 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief tooltip flight number now always current — tooltip content was built at mouseenter time, so if dep/arr changed within the 1-second delay window the displayed flight number was stale. The tooltip now uses a live-content callback (showLive) that evaluates the flight number at the moment the tooltip fires, not at hover start.
v2.12.3 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • SimBrief tooltip colon alignment corrected — the Aircraft label was one character short of the other labels, causing its colon to appear one position left of Origin, Dest, and Flt No.
v2.12.2 Apr 20, 2026 Improved
  • File on SimBrief tooltip — hovering the File on SimBrief button now shows a 1-second-delayed tooltip listing the origin, destination, aircraft type, and synthesized flight number that will be passed to SimBrief.
  • Flight number derivation uses ICAO-based owner lookup — the 3-letter owner code for each airport was previously read from the owner dropdown state, which could be stale or empty if the ICAO was entered by typing rather than via the matrix. The lookup now resolves the owner directly from the ICAO using the imported owner data, with the dropdown as fallback, matching the same pattern used elsewhere in the app.
v2.12.1 Apr 20, 2026 Fix
  • Suitability filter now re-evaluates when departure airport changes — the aircraft suitability filter was only applied when the filter button was clicked or a slider moved. Changing the departure airport (by typing in the field, clicking a matrix cell, or clicking a row label) did not re-run the range and runway checks, so arrival airports could remain dimmed or un-dimmed based on a stale departure. All three departure-change paths now call _distApplyFilter() after updating the field.
  • Aircraft type change no longer breaks suitability dimming — _distApplyFilter() was called before renderDistMatrix() in _acSelect(), so the dimming was applied to the old DOM and then immediately discarded when the table was rebuilt. Call order corrected so the filter runs after the fresh DOM exists.
v2.12.0 Apr 20, 2026 Feature
  • Footer standardized across all four SimFly apps — footer link renamed from "Active Player-Owned Airports Map" to "SimFly Active Player-Owned Airports" and recolored to the accent color; visited-state color is now the same as the default so the link never shifts appearance after being clicked.
v2.11.0 Apr 20, 2026 Feature
  • UTC/Local clock overlay — a fixed, always-visible widget in the top-left corner shows both local and UTC date/time in 24-hour Courier New format. Useful when planning flights or reviewing departure times since the app operates on UTC throughout.
v2.10.23 Apr 19, 2026 Improved
  • Aircraft suitability filter auto-activates on aircraft selection — the "Filter airports unsuitable for aircraft" button is now enabled automatically whenever an aircraft is selected, so runway and range suitability dimming applies live for all aircraft as fuel or payload is adjusted.
v2.10.22 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Reverted premature deferral of matrix dimming — restored live _distApplyFilter calls on fuel and payload slider input after the prior change incorrectly suppressed all live dimming.
v2.10.21 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Asterisk moved to large aircraft name in spec card — the "*" now appears next to the bold aircraft name (e.g. "Beechcraft Debonair 35 *"), not in the dropdown selector label.
v2.10.20 Apr 19, 2026 Improved
  • Asterisk added after selected aircraft name — indicates figures are estimates, paired with a new disclaimer above the Range scaling model link.
  • Planning disclaimer added to aircraft panel — "All figures are rough estimates for planning purposes only — use SimBrief for more accurate final calculations."
v2.10.19 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Matrix no longer dims all figures during fuel slider adjustment — range check is now positioned after the no-runway-constraints early return, so aircraft without runway length requirements are not incorrectly range-tested against the fuel-adjusted range mid-drag.
v2.10.18 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Out-of-range arrivals now dim correctly even without runway data — distance suitability is evaluated before checking for runway data, so airports beyond aircraft range are always shown as unsuitable when a departure is selected, regardless of whether OurAirports has runway records for that airport.
v2.10.17 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Runway surface codes translated to plain English — added missing OurAirports codes (GRE, TAR, CLA, SAN, PER, COM, UNK and others) to the surface lookup table so runway surface descriptions always display human-readable text rather than raw data codes.
v2.10.16 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Weights bar tooltip no longer occluded by panel elements — tooltip is now rendered at the document body level with fixed positioning, escaping the overflow:hidden clipping from parent aircraft card containers.
v2.10.15 Apr 19, 2026 Improved
  • Arrival tooltip landing line reworded — the runway required annotation for arrival airports now reads "with 10% fuel reserve" instead of "@ X% LDW", making the basis of the calculation plain.
  • Weights bar hover tooltip — hovering over the Weights bar row shows a Courier New numeric breakdown: OEW, Fuel loaded, Payload, TOW, and weight margin (or overage warning).
  • Fuel % and payload labels now use Courier New — the slider percentage readouts for fuel load and payload changed from Orbitron to Courier New for better numeric legibility.
v2.10.14 Apr 19, 2026 Fix
  • Fuel reserve is 10% of max fuel capacity, not 10% of loaded fuel — the reserve is now a fixed constant per airframe (10% of the aircraft's maximum usable fuel), rather than scaling with the fuel slider.
